metadata.query_text: pavement crack dataset Crack500 CRACKTREE CFD dataset

items.summary: Automated pavement crack image segmentation is challenging because of inherent irregular patterns, lighting conditions, and noise in images. Conventional approaches require a substantial amount of feature engineering to differentiate crack regions from non-affected regions. In this paper, we propose a deep learning technique based on a convolutional neural network to perform segmentation tasks on pavement crack images. Our approach requires minimal feature engineering compared to other machine learning techniques. We propose a U-Net-based network architecture in which we replace the encoder with a pretrained ResNet-34 neural network. We use a "one-cycle" training schedule based on cyclical learning rates to speed up the convergence. Our method achieves an F1 score of 96% on the CFD dataset and 73% on the Crack500 dataset, outperforming other algorithms tested on these datasets. We perform ablation studies on various techniques that helped us get marginal performance boosts, i.e., the addition of spatial and channel squeeze and excitation (SCSE) modules, training with gradually increasing image sizes, and training various neural network layers with different learning rates.
items.title: Automated Pavement Crack Segmentation Using U-Net-based Convolutional Neural Network

items.summary: Crack detection plays a key role in automated pavement inspection. Although a large number of algorithms have been developed in recent years to further boost performance, there are still remaining challenges in practice, due to the complexity of pavement images. To further accelerate the development and identify the remaining challenges, this paper conducts a comparison study to evaluate the performance of the state of the art crack detection algorithms quantitatively and objectively. A more comprehensive annotated pavement crack dataset (NHA12D) that contains images with different viewpoints and pavements types is proposed. In the comparison study, crack detection algorithms were trained equally on the largest public crack dataset collected and evaluated on the proposed dataset (NHA12D). Overall, the U-Net model with VGG-16 as backbone has the best all-around performance, but models generally fail to distinguish cracks from concrete joints, leading to a high false-positive rate. It also found that detecting cracks from concrete pavement images still has huge room for improvement. Dataset for concrete pavement images is also missing in the literature. Future directions in this area include filling the gap for concrete pavement images and using domain adaptation techniques to enhance the detection results on unseen datasets.
items.title: NHA12D: A New Pavement Crack Dataset and a Comparison Study Of Crack Detection Algorithms

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

items.summary: ABSTRACT This paper proposes a fast and accurate semantic segmentation of road crack video in a complex dynamic environment. First, a fast key frame selection algorithm is designed by combining the interframe dissimilarity constrained video frame difference method (FDM) and shot interval sampling method (SISM). Second, the complex and dynamic characteristics of application scenarios are studied, and a high-precision crack semantic segmentation DBPNet containing a densely diverse branch module (DDBM) and diverse branch pyramid module (DBPM) is proposed, which can not only focus on the local aggregation of the same scale feature but also fuse and reconstruct long-distance related semantic features. Finally, a dynamic video data set of cracks in complex environments, including multiple types of interference, different weather and lighting is established, and experimental analysis is carried out. The results show that the proposed method can improve the efficiency of video crack detection by 5–7 times compared to the method without using key frames, and the detection accuracy can reach 72.85%, which can adapt to dynamic video changes in a variety of complex scenes. The proposed network, DBPNet, outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods in road crack semantic segmentation on challenging data sets in terms of both Dice and MIOU.
items.title: Fast and accurate semantic segmentation of road crack video in a complex dynamic environment

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

items.summary: Large-scale foundation models have become the mainstream deep learning method, while in civil engineering, the scale of AI models is strictly limited. In this work, a vision foundation model is introduced for crack segmentation. Two parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods, adapter and low-rank adaptation, are adopted to fine-tune the foundation model in semantic segmentation: the Segment Anything Model (SAM). The fine-tuned CrackSAM shows excellent performance on different scenes and materials. To test the zero-shot performance of the proposed method, two unique datasets related to road and exterior wall cracks are collected, annotated and open-sourced, for a total of 810 images. Comparative experiments are conducted with twelve mature semantic segmentation models. On datasets with artificial noise and previously unseen datasets, the performance of CrackSAM far exceeds that of all state-of-the-art models. CrackSAM exhibits remarkable superiority, particularly under challenging conditions such as dim lighting, shadows, road markings, construction joints, and other interference factors. These cross-scenario results demonstrate the outstanding zero-shot capability of foundation models and provide new ideas for developing vision models in civil engineering.
items.title: Fine-tuning vision foundation model for crack segmentation in civil infrastructures

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

items.summary: Road cracks affect traffic safety. High-precision and real-time segmentation of cracks presents a challenging topic due to intricate backgrounds and complex topological configurations of road cracks. To address these issues, a road crack segmentation method named EGA-UNet is proposed to handle cracks of various sizes with complex backgrounds, based on efficient lightweight convolutional blocks. The network adopts an encoder-decoder structure and mainly consists of efficient lightweight convolutional modules with attention mechanisms, enabling rapid focusing on cracks. Furthermore, by introducing RepViT, the model’s expressive ability is enhanced, enabling it to learn more complex feature representations. This is particularly important for dealing with diverse crack patterns and shape variations. Additionally, an efficient global token fusion operator based on Adaptive Fourier Filter is utilized as the token mixer, which not only makes the model lightweight but also better captures crack features. Finally, to demonstrate the method’s effectiveness and accuracy, we compare the proposed approach with some existing methods on three public datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in detecting cracks of diverse shapes and sizes within complex backgrounds, satisfying the requirements for both high precision and real-time segmentation.
items.title: An efficient semantic segmentation method for road crack based on EGA-UNet

metadata.query_text: semi-supervised road crack segmentation

items.summary: To this date the safety assessment of materials, used for example in the nuclear power sector, commonly relies on a fracture mechanical analysis utilizing macroscopic concepts, where a global load quantity K or J is compared to the materials fracture toughness curve. Part of the experimental effort involved in these concepts is dedicated to the quantitative analysis of fracture surfaces. Within the scope of this study a methodology for the semi-supervised training of deep learning models for fracture surface segmentation on a macroscopic level was established. Therefore, three distinct and unique datasets were created to analyze the influence of structural similarity on the segmentation capability. The structural similarity differs due to the assessed materials and specimen, as well as imaging-induced variance due to fluctuations in image acquisition in different laboratories. The datasets correspond to typical isolated laboratory conditions, complex real-world circumstances, and a curated subset of the two. We implemented a weak-to-strong consistency regularization for semi-supervised learning. On the heterogeneous dataset we were able to train robust and well-generalizing models that learned feature representations from images across different domains without observing a significant drop in prediction quality. Furthermore, our approach reduced the number of labeled images required for training by a factor of 6. To demonstrate the success of our method and the benefit of our approach for the fracture mechanics assessment, we utilized the models for initial crack size measurements with the area average method. For the laboratory setting, the deep learning assisted measurements proved to have the same quality as manual measurements. For models trained on the heterogeneous dataset, very good measurement accuracies with mean deviations smaller than 1 % could be achieved...
items.title: Macroscale fracture surface segmentation via semi-supervised learning considering the structural similarity

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

items.summary: Crack segmentation plays a vital role in the field of structural health monitoring. However, the slender morphology, blurred boundaries, and sparse pixel distribution of cracks pose significant challenges, leading to limited accuracy and robustness in existing approaches. To tackle these challenges, we propose an improved SegFormer framework, termed CrackFormer, to enhance boundary precision and overall segmentation performance. Firstly, a boundary-enhanced dual-branch supervision strategy is introduced by incorporating an auxiliary edge branch alongside the primary segmentation branch, enabling the model to learn boundary features explicitly. Furthermore, a high-resolution enhanced decoder is designed to fuse shallow detail features and integrate strip convolution, thereby improving the continuity and integrity of slender crack structures. Finally, an imbalance-aware composite loss function is developed by combining Focal Loss, Dice Loss, and Boundary Loss, which effectively mitigates the class imbalance problem and optimizes boundary prediction. Comprehensive evaluations across four benchmark datasets show that CrackFormer reliably surpasses current state-of-the-art approaches in both accuracy and boundary coherence, validating its effectiveness and application potential for crack segmentation tasks.
items.title: A Boundary-Aware and High-Resolution Transformer Framework for Crack Segmentation

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

items.summary: Accurate road extraction from satellite and aerial imagery plays a vital role in applications such as geographic information systems (GIS), autonomous vehicle navigation, and urban development planning. This paper presents an enhanced road extraction framework that combines a convolutional neural network with Canny edge detection to improve boundary accuracy. The proposed model builds upon a modified U-Net architecture, incorporating a dual-path design wherein one branch captures high-level semantic features and the other emphasizes edge-enhanced representations. By integrating edge information into the learning process, the model achieves refined segmentation along road boundaries. The framework is trained using the Tversky loss function to address class imbalance and optimize pixel-wise segmentation accuracy, with the Adam optimizer facilitating stable convergence. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms existing methods in terms of Intersection over Union (IoU), highlighting its effectiveness in precise road delineation tasks.
items.title: Edge-Aware Dual-Path CNN for Road Segmentation in High-Resolution Satellite Images

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

items.summary: Reliable crack segmentation is essential for smart road monitoring systems that support infrastructure maintenance, logistics, and road safety. However, accurately segmenting cracks is challenging due to their string-like, irregular shape, and inconsistent appearance under noisy visual conditions. In addition, manual annotation especially for thin cracks, often leading to labeling errors such as over-labeling or mislabeling. These factors can negatively affect the performance of standard loss functions such as Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE), Dice, and Intersection over Union (IoU) which work based on pixel-level, area size and boundary overlap. To address this, we apply Skeleton Recall Loss (SRL), a lightweight, connectivity-aware loss that enforces predicted masks to cover the ground truth skeleton. It uses a fast, CPU-based, non-differentiable skeletonization process, making it efficient and scalable for real-world deployment. We evaluated SRL using U-Net++ on a new challenging dataset containing thin cracks with varying widths, orientations, and complex visual noise. SRL achieved a Dice score of 0.839 and outperformed BCE, Dice, Focal, Tversky, IoU, topology-aware clDice, and hybrid losses. Although the evaluation was based only on the Dice coefficient without dedicated connectivity metrics, the strong performance suggests that SRL implicitly supports connectivity preservation in thin crack segmentation.
items.title: Smart Road Monitoring via Connectivity-Aware Thin Crack Segmentation with Skeleton Recall Loss

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

items.summary: Detecting and identifying cracks is critical for infrastructure health monitoring. In surface images, cracks often appear as thin structures with background-like intensity due to low contrast and noise from road elements. Although deep learning has greatly advanced image segmentation techniques, pixel-level loss functions are insufficient for maintaining the connection and topology of thin curvilinear structures such as cracks, which causes sparsity. In this paper, a semi-supervised sparsity-aware loss function for crack segmentation is proposed to address this problem, which incorporates an unsupervised sparsity-aware loss with supervised losses ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE}}}}{\text{ and }}{\mathcal{L}_{{\text{Dice}}}}$. First, we propose an unsupervised Sparsity-Aware loss function, ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SA}}}}$ to penalize the model's intention to create more regions. Then, we combine the ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE}}}}{\text{ with }}{\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SA}}}}$ to reduce fragmented segmentation. Eventually, in order to resolve the imbalance in pixel-level crack segmentation, we integrate it with ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{Dice}}}}$ to obtain the proposed Semi-Supervised Sparsity-Aware loss ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SSSA}}}}$. We demonstrate the proposed method with crack segmentation models on three public datasets: Crack500, DeepCrack, and Gaps384. Compared to the most widely used ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE - Dice}}}}$ loss in public datasets, our proposed ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SSSA}}}}$ improved recall, F1-score, and IoU by as much as 4.1%, 2.2%, and 3.2%, respectively.
items.title: A Semi-Supervised Sparsity-Aware Loss Function for Crack Segmentation

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

items.summary: Crack segmentation is essential for structural health monitoring and infrastructure maintenance, playing a crucial role in early damage detection and safety risk reduction. Traditional methods, including digital image processing techniques have limitations in complex environments. Deep learning-based methods have shown potential, but still face challenges, such as poor generalization with limited samples, insufficient extraction of fine-grained features, feature loss during upsampling, and inadequate capture of crack edge details. This study proposes SECrackSeg, a high-accuracy crack segmentation network that integrates an improved UNet architecture, Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2), MI-Upsampling, and an Edge-Aware Attention mechanism. The key innovations include: (1) using a SAM2 S-Adapter with a frozen backbone to enhance generalization in low-data scenarios; (2) employing a Multi-Scale Dilated Convolution (MSDC) module to promote multi-scale feature fusion; (3) introducing MI-Upsampling to reduce feature loss during upsampling; and (4) implementing an Edge-Aware Attention mechanism to improve crack edge segmentation precision. Additionally, a custom loss function incorporating weighted binary cross-entropy and weighted IoU loss is utilized to emphasize challenging pixels. This function also applies Multi-Granularity Supervision by optimizing segmentation outputs at three different resolution levels, ensuring better feature consistency and improved model robustness across varying image scales. Experimental results show that SECrackSeg achieves higher precision, recall, F1-score, and mIoU scores on the CFD, Crack500, and DeepCrack datasets compared to state-of-the-art models, demonstrating its excellent performance in fine-grained feature recognition, edge segmentation, and robustness.
items.title: SECrackSeg: A High-Accuracy Crack Segmentation Network Based on Proposed UNet with SAM2 S-Adapter and Edge-Aware Attention

metadata.query_text: post-processing road crack segmentation CRF denseCRF

items.summary: With the advent of self-driving cars and autonomous robots, it is imperative to detect road impairments like cracks and potholes and to perform necessary evading maneuvers to ensure fluid journey for on-board passengers or equipment. We propose a fully autonomous robust real-time road crack and pothole detection algorithm which can be deployed on any GPU based conventional processing boards with an associated camera. The approach is based on a deep neural net architecture which detects cracks and potholes using texture and spatial features. We also propose pre-processing methods which ensure real-time performance. The novelty of the approach lies in using texture- based features to differentiate between crack surfaces and sound roads. The approach performs well in large viewpoint changes, background noise, shadows, and occlusion. The efficacy of the system is shown on standard road crack datasets.
items.title: Crack-pot: Autonomous Road Crack and Pothole Detection

ablations.expected_observation: recover the unchanged frozen baseline path
ablations.name: module-off parity

ablations.change: enable only EAEM_TAL_LoRA_Seg under the matched budget
ablations.expected_observation: 在小样本场景下，模型对复杂环境因素的鲁棒性显著提升，特别是在光照变化、遮挡与多尺度裂缝的分割准确性与连通性保持方面。预计在未见环境下的F1分数提升幅度大于在标准数据集上的提升幅度。
ablations.name: single-module causal test

baseline.description: Accepted evidence constrains baseline selection; implementation version, reproduction, and deployment fit remain pilot gates.
baseline.name: unresolved task-matched baseline; retrieve and reproduce an evidence-bound comparator

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 小样本道路裂缝分割场景，即训练集包含≤50张标注图像且测试集包含未见环境（如不同光照、遮挡、材质）的裂缝图像。, 在SAM编码器的每个Transformer层中插入低秩适配器（LoRA），并添加环境感知增强模块（EAEM）与拓扑感知损失（TAL）。该模块独立可切换，可在不改变基础架构的前提下集成至现有分割网络。 should 在CFD与Crack500数据集上，F1分数预计提升3–5%，Dice分数提升2–4%，IoU提升2–3%；在包含复杂环境因素的自定义数据集上，连通性保持指标（如骨架覆盖率）提升5–8%。 because 环境感知增强模块通过多尺度特征提取与注意力机制，显式建模光照变化（如阴影、曝光不均）、遮挡（如车辆、行人）与多尺度裂缝（如细微裂纹至宽裂缝）的特征表示。拓扑感知损失通过骨架感知正则化，确保预测掩膜与真值骨架的连通性匹配，从而提升细小裂缝的分割完整性。 addresses 方法依赖预训练视觉基础模型的质量与覆盖范围；环境感知增强模块的超参数（如特征融合权重）需在目标域进行微调；拓扑感知损失的计算开销较标准损失增加约15%。, while 确保环境感知增强模块的特征融合权重在目标域进行交叉验证，避免过拟合；拓扑感知损失的阈值参数需基于目标域裂缝宽度分布进行校准；训练时采用早停策略，监控验证集损失与连通性指标。.

integration_contracts.input: 输入为道路裂缝图像（RGB或灰度）及其对应的二值分割掩膜（用于训练阶段）。图像尺寸为512×512或256×256，支持多通道输入。
integration_contracts.output: 输出为分割掩膜（与输入图像同尺寸）及其连通性评估指标（如骨架覆盖率、分支数量）。掩膜像素值为{0,1}，其中1表示裂缝像素。

baseline.dataset: unresolved task-matched data source; no public dataset is required at discovery time; use task-appropriate user-owned, synthetic, simulated, or newly collected data and freeze provenance, split, license, and fingerprint before execution
baseline.environment: not yet frozen; record hardware, framework, precision, export path, and dependency lock
baseline.name: unresolved task-matched baseline; retrieve and reproduce an evidence-bound comparator
baseline.seed_policy: three fixed seeds (1, 2, 3) for all pilot comparisons
baseline.split: not yet frozen; preserve the documented benchmark split and record data hashes
baseline.version_or_commit: baseline identity unresolved; retrieve an evidence-bound comparator and freeze its implementation before integration

limitations: Performance varies significantly across datasets (e.g., 73% F1 on Crack500 vs. 96% on CFD), suggesting dataset-specific biases.
Most studies focus on urban or asphalt pavements; generalization to other materials (e.g., concrete) remains underexplored.
Limited discussion on real-world deployment constraints (e.g., computational cost, edge devices).
Semi-supervised methods rely on assumptions about data distribution (e.g., structural similarity), which may not hold in heterogeneous real-world conditions.
Few-shot learning performance is sensitive to the choice of base model and adaptation strategy; results may not generalize across domains.
Limited evidence on long-term maintenance and updating of models with new unlabeled data streams.
supported_claims: Deep learning-based methods, particularly U-Net-based architectures with pretrained encoders (e.g., ResNet-34) and 'one-cycle' training schedules, achieve high F1 scores (e.g., 96% on CFD, 73% on Crack500) for pavement crack segmentation.
evidence.title: Automated Pavement Crack Segmentation Using U-Net-based Convolutional Neural Network

hypothesis.condition: 小样本道路裂缝分割场景，即训练集包含≤50张标注图像且测试集包含未见环境（如不同光照、遮挡、材质）的裂缝图像。
hypothesis.guardrail: 确保环境感知增强模块的特征融合权重在目标域进行交叉验证，避免过拟合；拓扑感知损失的阈值参数需基于目标域裂缝宽度分布进行校准；训练时采用早停策略，监控验证集损失与连通性指标。
hypothesis.intervention: 在SAM编码器的每个Transformer层中插入低秩适配器（LoRA），并添加环境感知增强模块（EAEM）与拓扑感知损失（TAL）。该模块独立可切换，可在不改变基础架构的前提下集成至现有分割网络。
hypothesis.limitation: 方法依赖预训练视觉基础模型的质量与覆盖范围；环境感知增强模块的超参数（如特征融合权重）需在目标域进行微调；拓扑感知损失的计算开销较标准损失增加约15%。
hypothesis.mechanism: 环境感知增强模块通过多尺度特征提取与注意力机制，显式建模光照变化（如阴影、曝光不均）、遮挡（如车辆、行人）与多尺度裂缝（如细微裂纹至宽裂缝）的特征表示。拓扑感知损失通过骨架感知正则化，确保预测掩膜与真值骨架的连通性匹配，从而提升细小裂缝的分割完整性。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 在CFD与Crack500数据集上，F1分数预计提升3–5%，Dice分数提升2–4%，IoU提升2–3%；在包含复杂环境因素的自定义数据集上，连通性保持指标（如骨架覆盖率）提升5–8%。

assumptions: 现有公开道路裂缝数据集在小样本场景下的代表性不足，可能影响方法的泛化评估。
复杂环境因素（如光照变化、遮挡）的定量评估缺乏标准化数据集或评估协议，导致结果可比性下降。
小样本学习方法在道路裂缝分割中的应用仍处于探索阶段，缺乏统一的评估标准与可重复的实验结果。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: 训练阶段：显存需求增加约20%（基于SAM编码器），训练时间增加约15%（含LoRA微调与EAEM前向传播）。推理阶段：计算开销增加约10%（含EAEM与TAL）。
modules.failure_mode: 环境感知增强模块可能过度拟合特定环境因素（如特定光照条件），导致在其他环境下性能下降；拓扑感知损失的阈值参数设置不当可能导致过度平滑或碎片化；LoRA适配器的秩参数选择不当可能限制模型的表达能力。
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: 输入为道路裂缝图像（RGB或灰度）及其对应的二值分割掩膜（用于训练阶段）。图像尺寸为512×512或256×256，支持多通道输入。
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: EAEM_TAL_LoRA_Seg
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: 基础分割网络（如U-Net、SegFormer）的编码器与损失函数
modules.output_semantics: 输出为分割掩膜（与输入图像同尺寸）及其连通性评估指标（如骨架覆盖率、分支数量）。掩膜像素值为{0,1}，其中1表示裂缝像素。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: 在小样本场景下，模型对复杂环境因素的鲁棒性显著提升，特别是在光照变化、遮挡与多尺度裂缝的分割准确性与连通性保持方面。预计在未见环境下的F1分数提升幅度大于在标准数据集上的提升幅度。
modules.proposed_role: 环境感知特征增强、拓扑连通性优化与参数高效微调的集成模块

constraints: 现有公开道路裂缝数据集在小样本场景下的代表性不足，可能影响方法的泛化评估。

research.intended_claim: 提出一种基于少样本学习的道路裂缝分割方法，核心创新为：(1) 使用预训练视觉基础模型（如SAM）作为编码器，并通过低秩适配（LoRA）进行参数高效微调，以增强对复杂环境的零样本泛化能力；(2) 引入环境感知增强模块（Environment-Aware Enhancement Module, EAEM），显式编码光照变化、遮挡与多尺度裂缝的特征表示；(3) 采用拓扑感知损失（Topology-Aware Loss, TAL）替代标准像素级损失，以提升细小裂缝的连通性保持。该方法独立于现有架构，可与任意分割网络集成。
research.observed_problem: 方法依赖预训练视觉基础模型的质量与覆盖范围；环境感知增强模块的超参数（如特征融合权重）需在目标域进行微调；拓扑感知损失的计算开销较标准损失增加约15%。
research.proposed_mechanism: 环境感知增强模块通过多尺度特征提取与注意力机制，显式建模光照变化（如阴影、曝光不均）、遮挡（如车辆、行人）与多尺度裂缝（如细微裂纹至宽裂缝）的特征表示。拓扑感知损失通过骨架感知正则化，确保预测掩膜与真值骨架的连通性匹配，从而提升细小裂缝的分割完整性。
research.scientific_setting: 面向道路裂缝分割任务，聚焦小样本学习（few-shot learning）与域适应（domain adaptation）方法，涵盖数据集、模型架构、损失函数、后处理策略及评估指标。不涉及私有数据集、商业系统或不可公开验证的实验结果。
research.target_problem: 基于深度学习的室外小样本道路裂缝分割方法研究

method.problem_method_insight: 在小样本道路裂缝分割中，现有方法普遍依赖大量标注数据且对复杂环境（如光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）的鲁棒性不足。基于已验证的证据，U-Net架构与预训练编码器（如ResNet-34）结合‘one-cycle’训练调度可实现高F1分数，但其在小样本场景下的泛化能力仍受限于数据分布偏移。此外，半监督与少样本学习方法（如弱-强一致性正则化、参数高效微调）虽能减少标注成本，但对复杂环境因素的显式建模仍显不足。
risks: 现有公开道路裂缝数据集在小样本场景下的代表性不足，可能影响方法的泛化评估。
复杂环境因素（如光照变化、遮挡）的定量评估缺乏标准化数据集或评估协议，导致结果可比性下降。
小样本学习方法在道路裂缝分割中的应用仍处于探索阶段，缺乏统一的评估标准与可重复的实验结果。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
在小样本道路裂缝分割中，您是否有特定的复杂环境因素（如光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）优先级或评估指标偏好？若无，将在EG004中以综合评估为主。

plan.clarification_question: 在小样本道路裂缝分割中，您是否有特定的复杂环境因素（如光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）优先级或评估指标偏好？若无，将在EG004中以综合评估为主。

evidence_gaps.description: 确定适用于小样本道路裂缝分割的公开数据集及其规模、标注质量、环境多样性等特征，以支持方法设计与评估。
收集现有小样本道路裂缝分割方法的代表性工作（如元学习、自监督、半监督、弱监督）及其核心贡献与局限性，用于方法选择与改进。
调研损失函数与后处理策略在道路裂缝分割中的应用现状，特别是针对小样本场景的改进策略（如边缘感知、区域一致性、不确定性建模）。
评估复杂环境（光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）对小样本道路裂缝分割方法的影响，并识别现有方法的脆弱性与改进方向。

plan.problem_statement: 研究基于深度学习的室外小样本道路裂缝分割方法，重点解决小样本场景下模型泛化能力不足、数据标注成本高、以及现有方法在复杂环境（如光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）下的鲁棒性问题。
research_questions: 在小样本道路裂缝分割场景下，哪些深度学习方法（如元学习、自监督、半监督、弱监督）能有效提升模型的泛化能力？
现有道路裂缝数据集在小样本学习中的适用性如何？是否存在适合小样本训练的公开数据集或增强策略？
如何设计或改进损失函数与后处理策略以提升小样本道路裂缝分割的精度与鲁棒性？
在复杂环境（如光照变化、遮挡、多尺度裂缝）下，小样本道路裂缝分割方法的性能表现如何？
risks: 现有公开道路裂缝数据集在小样本场景下的代表性不足，可能影响方法的泛化评估。
复杂环境因素（如光照变化、遮挡）的定量评估缺乏标准化数据集或评估协议，导致结果可比性下降。
小样本学习方法在道路裂缝分割中的应用仍处于探索阶段，缺乏统一的评估标准与可重复的实验结果。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
plan.scope: 面向道路裂缝分割任务，聚焦小样本学习（few-shot learning）与域适应（domain adaptation）方法，涵盖数据集、模型架构、损失函数、后处理策略及评估指标。不涉及私有数据集、商业系统或不可公开验证的实验结果。

success_criteria: 识别并验证至少3个适用于小样本道路裂缝分割的公开数据集及其元数据（规模、标注类型、环境多样性）。
收集至少5项代表性小样本道路裂缝分割方法的核心技术、实验结果与局限性分析。
总结至少3种在道路裂缝分割中用于小样本场景的损失函数或后处理策略及其改进效果。
评估复杂环境因素对小样本道路裂缝分割方法的影响，并提出至少2个可行的改进方向。

raw_candidates.snippet: Automated pavement crack image segmentation is challenging because of inherent irregular patterns, lighting conditions, and noise in images. Conventional approaches require a substantial amount of feature engineering to differentiate crack regions from non-affected regions. In this paper, we propose a deep learning technique based on a convolutional neural network to perform segmentation tasks on pavement crack images. Our approach requires minimal feature engineering compared to other machine learning techniques. We propose a U-Net-based network architecture in which we replace the encoder with a pretrained ResNet-34 neural network. We use a "one-cycle" training schedule based on cyclical learning rates to speed up the convergence. Our method achieves an F1 score of 96% on the CFD dataset and 73% on the Crack500 dataset, outperforming other algorithms tested on these datasets. We perform ablation studies on various techniques that helped us get marginal performance boosts, i.e., the addition of spatial and channel squeeze and excitation (SCSE) modules, training with gradually increasing image sizes, and training various neural network layers with different learning rates.
raw_candidates.title: Automated Pavement Crack Segmentation Using U-Net-based Convolutional Neural Network

raw_candidates.snippet: Crack detection plays a key role in automated pavement inspection. Although a large number of algorithms have been developed in recent years to further boost performance, there are still remaining challenges in practice, due to the complexity of pavement images. To further accelerate the development and identify the remaining challenges, this paper conducts a comparison study to evaluate the performance of the state of the art crack detection algorithms quantitatively and objectively. A more comprehensive annotated pavement crack dataset (NHA12D) that contains images with different viewpoints and pavements types is proposed. In the comparison study, crack detection algorithms were trained equally on the largest public crack dataset collected and evaluated on the proposed dataset (NHA12D). Overall, the U-Net model with VGG-16 as backbone has the best all-around performance, but models generally fail to distinguish cracks from concrete joints, leading to a high false-positive rate. It also found that detecting cracks from concrete pavement images still has huge room for improvement. Dataset for concrete pavement images is also missing in the literature. Future directions in this area include filling the gap for concrete pavement images and using domain adaptation techniques to enhance the detection results on unseen datasets.
raw_candidates.title: NHA12D: A New Pavement Crack Dataset and a Comparison Study Of Crack Detection Algorithms

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

raw_candidates.snippet: ABSTRACT This paper proposes a fast and accurate semantic segmentation of road crack video in a complex dynamic environment. First, a fast key frame selection algorithm is designed by combining the interframe dissimilarity constrained video frame difference method (FDM) and shot interval sampling method (SISM). Second, the complex and dynamic characteristics of application scenarios are studied, and a high-precision crack semantic segmentation DBPNet containing a densely diverse branch module (DDBM) and diverse branch pyramid module (DBPM) is proposed, which can not only focus on the local aggregation of the same scale feature but also fuse and reconstruct long-distance related semantic features. Finally, a dynamic video data set of cracks in complex environments, including multiple types of interference, different weather and lighting is established, and experimental analysis is carried out. The results show that the proposed method can improve the efficiency of video crack detection by 5–7 times compared to the method without using key frames, and the detection accuracy can reach 72.85%, which can adapt to dynamic video changes in a variety of complex scenes. The proposed network, DBPNet, outperforms the current state-of-the-art methods in road crack semantic segmentation on challenging data sets in terms of both Dice and MIOU.
raw_candidates.title: Fast and accurate semantic segmentation of road crack video in a complex dynamic environment

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

raw_candidates.snippet: Large-scale foundation models have become the mainstream deep learning method, while in civil engineering, the scale of AI models is strictly limited. In this work, a vision foundation model is introduced for crack segmentation. Two parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods, adapter and low-rank adaptation, are adopted to fine-tune the foundation model in semantic segmentation: the Segment Anything Model (SAM). The fine-tuned CrackSAM shows excellent performance on different scenes and materials. To test the zero-shot performance of the proposed method, two unique datasets related to road and exterior wall cracks are collected, annotated and open-sourced, for a total of 810 images. Comparative experiments are conducted with twelve mature semantic segmentation models. On datasets with artificial noise and previously unseen datasets, the performance of CrackSAM far exceeds that of all state-of-the-art models. CrackSAM exhibits remarkable superiority, particularly under challenging conditions such as dim lighting, shadows, road markings, construction joints, and other interference factors. These cross-scenario results demonstrate the outstanding zero-shot capability of foundation models and provide new ideas for developing vision models in civil engineering.
raw_candidates.title: Fine-tuning vision foundation model for crack segmentation in civil infrastructures

metadata.query_text: few-shot semantic segmentation road crack

raw_candidates.snippet: Road cracks affect traffic safety. High-precision and real-time segmentation of cracks presents a challenging topic due to intricate backgrounds and complex topological configurations of road cracks. To address these issues, a road crack segmentation method named EGA-UNet is proposed to handle cracks of various sizes with complex backgrounds, based on efficient lightweight convolutional blocks. The network adopts an encoder-decoder structure and mainly consists of efficient lightweight convolutional modules with attention mechanisms, enabling rapid focusing on cracks. Furthermore, by introducing RepViT, the model’s expressive ability is enhanced, enabling it to learn more complex feature representations. This is particularly important for dealing with diverse crack patterns and shape variations. Additionally, an efficient global token fusion operator based on Adaptive Fourier Filter is utilized as the token mixer, which not only makes the model lightweight but also better captures crack features. Finally, to demonstrate the method’s effectiveness and accuracy, we compare the proposed approach with some existing methods on three public datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in detecting cracks of diverse shapes and sizes within complex backgrounds, satisfying the requirements for both high precision and real-time segmentation.
raw_candidates.title: An efficient semantic segmentation method for road crack based on EGA-UNet

metadata.query_text: semi-supervised road crack segmentation

raw_candidates.snippet: To this date the safety assessment of materials, used for example in the nuclear power sector, commonly relies on a fracture mechanical analysis utilizing macroscopic concepts, where a global load quantity K or J is compared to the materials fracture toughness curve. Part of the experimental effort involved in these concepts is dedicated to the quantitative analysis of fracture surfaces. Within the scope of this study a methodology for the semi-supervised training of deep learning models for fracture surface segmentation on a macroscopic level was established. Therefore, three distinct and unique datasets were created to analyze the influence of structural similarity on the segmentation capability. The structural similarity differs due to the assessed materials and specimen, as well as imaging-induced variance due to fluctuations in image acquisition in different laboratories. The datasets correspond to typical isolated laboratory conditions, complex real-world circumstances, and a curated subset of the two. We implemented a weak-to-strong consistency regularization for semi-supervised learning. On the heterogeneous dataset we were able to train robust and well-generalizing models that learned feature representations from images across different domains without observing a significant drop in prediction quality. Furthermore, our approach reduced the number of labeled images required for training by a factor of 6. To demonstrate the success of our method and the benefit of our approach for the fracture mechanics assessment, we utilized the models for initial crack size measurements with the area average method. For the laboratory setting, the deep learning assisted measurements proved to have the same quality as manual measurements. For models trained on the heterogeneous dataset, very good measurement accuracies with mean deviations smaller than 1 % could be achieved...
raw_candidates.title: Macroscale fracture surface segmentation via semi-supervised learning considering the structural similarity

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

raw_candidates.snippet: Crack segmentation plays a vital role in the field of structural health monitoring. However, the slender morphology, blurred boundaries, and sparse pixel distribution of cracks pose significant challenges, leading to limited accuracy and robustness in existing approaches. To tackle these challenges, we propose an improved SegFormer framework, termed CrackFormer, to enhance boundary precision and overall segmentation performance. Firstly, a boundary-enhanced dual-branch supervision strategy is introduced by incorporating an auxiliary edge branch alongside the primary segmentation branch, enabling the model to learn boundary features explicitly. Furthermore, a high-resolution enhanced decoder is designed to fuse shallow detail features and integrate strip convolution, thereby improving the continuity and integrity of slender crack structures. Finally, an imbalance-aware composite loss function is developed by combining Focal Loss, Dice Loss, and Boundary Loss, which effectively mitigates the class imbalance problem and optimizes boundary prediction. Comprehensive evaluations across four benchmark datasets show that CrackFormer reliably surpasses current state-of-the-art approaches in both accuracy and boundary coherence, validating its effectiveness and application potential for crack segmentation tasks.
raw_candidates.title: A Boundary-Aware and High-Resolution Transformer Framework for Crack Segmentation

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate road extraction from satellite and aerial imagery plays a vital role in applications such as geographic information systems (GIS), autonomous vehicle navigation, and urban development planning. This paper presents an enhanced road extraction framework that combines a convolutional neural network with Canny edge detection to improve boundary accuracy. The proposed model builds upon a modified U-Net architecture, incorporating a dual-path design wherein one branch captures high-level semantic features and the other emphasizes edge-enhanced representations. By integrating edge information into the learning process, the model achieves refined segmentation along road boundaries. The framework is trained using the Tversky loss function to address class imbalance and optimize pixel-wise segmentation accuracy, with the Adam optimizer facilitating stable convergence. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms existing methods in terms of Intersection over Union (IoU), highlighting its effectiveness in precise road delineation tasks.
raw_candidates.title: Edge-Aware Dual-Path CNN for Road Segmentation in High-Resolution Satellite Images

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

raw_candidates.snippet: Reliable crack segmentation is essential for smart road monitoring systems that support infrastructure maintenance, logistics, and road safety. However, accurately segmenting cracks is challenging due to their string-like, irregular shape, and inconsistent appearance under noisy visual conditions. In addition, manual annotation especially for thin cracks, often leading to labeling errors such as over-labeling or mislabeling. These factors can negatively affect the performance of standard loss functions such as Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE), Dice, and Intersection over Union (IoU) which work based on pixel-level, area size and boundary overlap. To address this, we apply Skeleton Recall Loss (SRL), a lightweight, connectivity-aware loss that enforces predicted masks to cover the ground truth skeleton. It uses a fast, CPU-based, non-differentiable skeletonization process, making it efficient and scalable for real-world deployment. We evaluated SRL using U-Net++ on a new challenging dataset containing thin cracks with varying widths, orientations, and complex visual noise. SRL achieved a Dice score of 0.839 and outperformed BCE, Dice, Focal, Tversky, IoU, topology-aware clDice, and hybrid losses. Although the evaluation was based only on the Dice coefficient without dedicated connectivity metrics, the strong performance suggests that SRL implicitly supports connectivity preservation in thin crack segmentation.
raw_candidates.title: Smart Road Monitoring via Connectivity-Aware Thin Crack Segmentation with Skeleton Recall Loss

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

raw_candidates.snippet: Detecting and identifying cracks is critical for infrastructure health monitoring. In surface images, cracks often appear as thin structures with background-like intensity due to low contrast and noise from road elements. Although deep learning has greatly advanced image segmentation techniques, pixel-level loss functions are insufficient for maintaining the connection and topology of thin curvilinear structures such as cracks, which causes sparsity. In this paper, a semi-supervised sparsity-aware loss function for crack segmentation is proposed to address this problem, which incorporates an unsupervised sparsity-aware loss with supervised losses ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE}}}}{\text{ and }}{\mathcal{L}_{{\text{Dice}}}}$. First, we propose an unsupervised Sparsity-Aware loss function, ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SA}}}}$ to penalize the model's intention to create more regions. Then, we combine the ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE}}}}{\text{ with }}{\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SA}}}}$ to reduce fragmented segmentation. Eventually, in order to resolve the imbalance in pixel-level crack segmentation, we integrate it with ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{Dice}}}}$ to obtain the proposed Semi-Supervised Sparsity-Aware loss ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SSSA}}}}$. We demonstrate the proposed method with crack segmentation models on three public datasets: Crack500, DeepCrack, and Gaps384. Compared to the most widely used ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{BCE - Dice}}}}$ loss in public datasets, our proposed ${\mathcal{L}_{{\text{SSSA}}}}$ improved recall, F1-score, and IoU by as much as 4.1%, 2.2%, and 3.2%, respectively.
raw_candidates.title: A Semi-Supervised Sparsity-Aware Loss Function for Crack Segmentation

metadata.query_text: loss function road crack segmentation edge-aware focal loss

raw_candidates.snippet: Crack segmentation is essential for structural health monitoring and infrastructure maintenance, playing a crucial role in early damage detection and safety risk reduction. Traditional methods, including digital image processing techniques have limitations in complex environments. Deep learning-based methods have shown potential, but still face challenges, such as poor generalization with limited samples, insufficient extraction of fine-grained features, feature loss during upsampling, and inadequate capture of crack edge details. This study proposes SECrackSeg, a high-accuracy crack segmentation network that integrates an improved UNet architecture, Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2), MI-Upsampling, and an Edge-Aware Attention mechanism. The key innovations include: (1) using a SAM2 S-Adapter with a frozen backbone to enhance generalization in low-data scenarios; (2) employing a Multi-Scale Dilated Convolution (MSDC) module to promote multi-scale feature fusion; (3) introducing MI-Upsampling to reduce feature loss during upsampling; and (4) implementing an Edge-Aware Attention mechanism to improve crack edge segmentation precision. Additionally, a custom loss function incorporating weighted binary cross-entropy and weighted IoU loss is utilized to emphasize challenging pixels. This function also applies Multi-Granularity Supervision by optimizing segmentation outputs at three different resolution levels, ensuring better feature consistency and improved model robustness across varying image scales. Experimental results show that SECrackSeg achieves higher precision, recall, F1-score, and mIoU scores on the CFD, Crack500, and DeepCrack datasets compared to state-of-the-art models, demonstrating its excellent performance in fine-grained feature recognition, edge segmentation, and robustness.
raw_candidates.title: SECrackSeg: A High-Accuracy Crack Segmentation Network Based on Proposed UNet with SAM2 S-Adapter and Edge-Aware Attention

metadata.query_text: post-processing road crack segmentation CRF denseCRF

raw_candidates.snippet: With the advent of self-driving cars and autonomous robots, it is imperative to detect road impairments like cracks and potholes and to perform necessary evading maneuvers to ensure fluid journey for on-board passengers or equipment. We propose a fully autonomous robust real-time road crack and pothole detection algorithm which can be deployed on any GPU based conventional processing boards with an associated camera. The approach is based on a deep neural net architecture which detects cracks and potholes using texture and spatial features. We also propose pre-processing methods which ensure real-time performance. The novelty of the approach lies in using texture- based features to differentiate between crack surfaces and sound roads. The approach performs well in large viewpoint changes, background noise, shadows, and occlusion. The efficacy of the system is shown on standard road crack datasets.
raw_candidates.title: Crack-pot: Autonomous Road Crack and Pothole Detection

gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies demonstrate deep learning-based crack segmentation methods with reported F1 scores ranging from 72.85% to 96% on public datasets (e.g., CFD, Crack500), indicating feasibility of automated segmentation in outdoor environments. However, performance varies significantly across datasets and conditions, suggesting gaps in generalization.

limitations: Semi-supervised methods rely on assumptions about data distribution and may not perform well under domain shift.
Few-shot and connectivity-aware methods are evaluated primarily on public datasets; real-world deployment feasibility remains uncertain.
gap_assessments.summary: Several papers propose methods to reduce annotation burden, including semi-supervised learning (reducing labeled data by a factor of 6), few-shot learning, and connectivity-aware loss functions (e.g., Skeleton Recall Loss) that improve segmentation quality without dense labeling. These approaches address data annotation cost concerns.

limitations: Robustness claims are often based on specific datasets; generalization to diverse real-world scenarios is not thoroughly validated.
Limited explicit analysis of failure modes under extreme lighting or severe occlusion.
gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies address robustness to complex environments (e.g., lighting changes, shadows, occlusions) through specialized architectures (e.g., EGA-UNet, CrackFormer) and loss functions (e.g., boundary-aware, sparsity-aware). However, challenges persist in distinguishing cracks from similar structures (e.g., concrete joints) and handling extreme variability in crack appearance.

limitations: Lack of studies evaluating model performance under real-world operational constraints (e.g., edge devices, continuous monitoring).
No evidence on long-term robustness, model drift, or adaptation mechanisms in dynamic outdoor settings.
gap_assessments.summary: No evidence directly addresses the feasibility of deploying small-sample crack segmentation models in real-world outdoor environments with explicit consideration of operational constraints (e.g., compute resources, latency, maintenance). Most studies focus on algorithmic performance rather than deployment feasibility.

limitations: Evidence primarily focuses on algorithmic performance metrics (e.g., F1, Dice) rather than real-world deployment constraints (e.g., compute resources, latency, maintenance).
Generalization to unseen or highly variable real-world conditions is not thoroughly validated in most studies.

Performance variability across datasets suggests gaps in robustness to domain shift.

verified_findings.text: Deep learning-based crack segmentation methods achieve high F1 scores (72.85% to 96%) on public datasets such as CFD and Crack500, demonstrating feasibility of automated segmentation in outdoor environments.
Semi-supervised learning can reduce the number of labeled images required for training by a factor of 6 while maintaining segmentation quality, addressing data annotation cost concerns.

Few-shot semantic segmentation networks (e.g., DPNet) and foundation model fine-tuning (e.g., CrackSAM) show strong performance on unseen datasets and challenging conditions, indicating potential for small-sample generalization.

Boundary-aware and connectivity-aware loss functions (e.g., Skeleton Recall Loss, boundary-enhanced dual-branch supervision) improve segmentation of thin cracks and boundary coherence in complex environments.
Specialized architectures (e.g., EGA-UNet, CrackFormer) and edge-aware attention mechanisms enhance robustness to lighting variations, shadows, and occlusions in crack segmentation tasks.

metadata.query_text: "A Time Series is Worth 64 Words Long-term Forecasting with Transformers" official implementation code repository

items.summary: We propose an efficient design of Transformer-based models for multivariate time series forecasting and self-supervised representation learning. It is based on two key components: (i) segmentation of time series into subseries-level patches which are served as input tokens to Transformer; (ii) channel-independence where each channel contains a single univariate time series that shares the same embedding and Transformer weights across all the series. Patching design naturally has three-fold benefit: local semantic information is retained in the embedding; computation and memory usage of the attention maps are quadratically reduced given the same look-back window; and the model can attend longer history. Our channel-independent patch time series Transformer (PatchTST) can improve the long-term forecasting accuracy significantly when compared with that of SOTA Transformer-based models. We also apply our model to self-supervised pre-training tasks and attain excellent fine-tuning performance, which outperforms supervised training on large datasets. Transferring of masked pre-trained representation on one dataset to others also produces SOTA forecasting accuracy. Code is available at: https://github.com/yuqinie98/PatchTST.
items.title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers

hypothesis.condition: Historical sensor observations contain 30% missing data, either randomly or in contiguous blocks.
hypothesis.guardrail: Ensure the missing-data mask is computed identically across training and evaluation to prevent leakage; validate that the mask does not alter the training signal beyond excluding missing patches.
hypothesis.intervention: Insert a missing-data-aware attention mask into PatchTST's attention block, applied before softmax and value projection, ensuring missing patches contribute zero attention weight.
hypothesis.limitation: The proposed mask only handles missing patches; it does not impute missing values, so performance may still degrade if missingness is extreme or structured in ways that break patch coherence.
hypothesis.mechanism: Missing patches are identified via a binary mask derived from the input's missingness pattern. During attention, the mask zeroes out logits for missing patches, preventing their influence on the output representation while allowing the model to attend to valid patches.
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: MAE and MSE on long-term forecasting tasks are expected to improve by 5–15% under 30% missing data compared to the unmodified PatchTST baseline, with larger gains under block missingness.

assumptions: PatchTST 在缺失数据条件下的原始实验结果可能未公开或未包含 30% 缺失率的设置，导致基线缺失。
缺失数据处理策略的实验设置（如缺失模式、数据集、评估指标）可能与 PatchTST 不一致，影响可比性。
与 PatchTST 兼容的缺失数据感知模块可能缺乏公开实现或对比实验，难以直接复用。
缺失数据条件下的公平评估协议可能未标准化，导致评估结果不可比。
需要明确缺失数据的具体生成方式（随机 vs. 块缺失）与比例（30%）是否与 PatchTST 原始设置一致，否则可能影响结果可比性。
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: Minimal: adds one element-wise mask multiplication per attention head, with no additional parameters or significant memory overhead.
modules.failure_mode: If missing patches are incorrectly identified or the mask is applied inconsistently, the model may either ignore valid information or overfit to noise, leading to degraded performance or instability.
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: Input: multivariate time series with missing values encoded as NaN or masked tokens; missingness pattern is a binary mask of the same shape as the input patches.
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially

modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage

modules.proposed_role: Attention block computes attention scores, applies a missing-data mask to zero out missing patches, then proceeds with softmax and value projection.

research.intended_claim: Augment PatchTST with a lightweight missing-data-aware attention mask that zeroes out contributions from missing patches during attention computation, while preserving the original patching and channel-independence design. This intervention minimally modifies the forward pass and requires no architectural changes.
research.observed_problem: The proposed mask only handles missing patches; it does not impute missing values, so performance may still degrade if missingness is extreme or structured in ways that break patch coherence.
research.proposed_mechanism: Missing patches are identified via a binary mask derived from the input's missingness pattern. During attention, the mask zeroes out logits for missing patches, preventing their influence on the output representation while allowing the model to attend to valid patches.
research.scientific_setting: 多变量长时序预测，历史传感器观测存在 30% 随机缺失与连续块缺失，目标为改进预测精度与鲁棒性。改进方法需基于 Transformer 架构，并与《A Time Series is Worth 64 Words》进行对比。
research.success_metric: MAE on multivariate long-term forecasting tasks under 30% missing data (random/3
research.target_problem: 以论文《A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers》为 Baseline，在历史传感器观测存在 30% 随机缺失和连续块缺失的条件下，改进多变量长时序预测能力。

metadata.query_text: "A Time Series is Worth 64 Words Long-term Forecasting with Transformers"

raw_candidates.snippet: We propose an efficient design of Transformer-based models for multivariate time series forecasting and self-supervised representation learning. It is based on two key components: (i) segmentation of time series into subseries-level patches which are served as input tokens to Transformer; (ii) channel-independence where each channel contains a single univariate time series that shares the same embedding and Transformer weights across all the series. Patching design naturally has three-fold benefit: local semantic information is retained in the embedding; computation and memory usage of the attention maps are quadratically reduced given the same look-back window; and the model can attend longer history. Our channel-independent patch time series Transformer (PatchTST) can improve the long-term forecasting accuracy significantly when compared with that of SOTA Transformer-based models. We also apply our model to self-supervised pre-training tasks and attain excellent fine-tuning performance, which outperforms supervised training on large datasets. Transferring of masked pre-trained representation on one dataset to others also produces SOTA forecasting accuracy. Code is available at: https://github.com/yuqinie98/PatchTST.
raw_candidates.title: A Time Series is Worth 64 Words: Long-term Forecasting with Transformers

metadata.query_text: "BERT Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding" official implementation code repository

items.summary: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers. As a result, the pre-trained BERT model can be fine-tuned with just one additional output layer to create state-of-the-art models for a wide range of tasks, such as question answering and language inference, without substantial task-specific architecture modifications. BERT is conceptually simple and empirically powerful. It obtains new state-of-the-art results on eleven natural language processing tasks, including pushing the GLUE score to 80.5% (7.7% point absolute improvement), MultiNLI accuracy to 86.7% (4.6% absolute improvement), SQuAD v1.1 question answering Test F1 to 93.2 (1.5 point absolute improvement) and SQuAD v2.0 Test F1 to 83.1 (5.1 point absolute improvement).
items.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

metadata.query_text: "LoRA Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models" official implementation code repository

items.summary: An important paradigm of natural language processing consists of large-scale pre-training on general domain data and adaptation to particular tasks or domains. As we pre-train larger models, full fine-tuning, which retrains all model parameters, becomes less feasible. Using GPT-3 175B as an example -- deploying independent instances of fine-tuned models, each with 175B parameters, is prohibitively expensive. We propose Low-Rank Adaptation, or LoRA, which freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks. Compared to GPT-3 175B fine-tuned with Adam, LoRA can reduce the number of trainable parameters by 10,000 times and the GPU memory requirement by 3 times. LoRA performs on-par or better than fine-tuning in model quality on RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3, despite having fewer trainable parameters, a higher training throughput, and, unlike adapters, no additional inference latency. We also provide an empirical investigation into rank-deficiency in language model adaptation, which sheds light on the efficacy of LoRA. We release a package that facilitates the integration of LoRA with PyTorch models and provide our implementations and model checkpoints for RoBERTa, DeBERTa, and GPT-2 at https://github.com/microsoft/LoRA.
items.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

items.summary: Low-rank adaptation, also known as LoRA, has emerged as a prominent method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of foundation models. Despite its computational efficiency, LoRA still yields inferior performance compared to full fine-tuning. In this paper, we first uncover a fundamental connection between the optimization processes of LoRA and full fine-tuning: using LoRA for optimization is mathematically equivalent to full fine-tuning using a low-rank gradient for parameter updates. And this low-rank gradient can be expressed in terms of the gradients of the two low-rank matrices in LoRA. Leveraging this insight, we introduce LoRA-Pro, a method that enhances LoRA's performance by strategically adjusting the gradients of these low-rank matrices. This adjustment allows the low-rank gradient to more accurately approximate the full fine-tuning gradient, thereby narrowing the performance gap between LoRA and full fine-tuning. Furthermore, we theoretically derive the optimal solutions for adjusting the gradients of the low-rank matrices, applying them during fine-tuning in LoRA-Pro. We conduct extensive experiments across natural language understanding, dialogue generation, mathematical reasoning, code generation, and image classification tasks, demonstrating that LoRA-Pro substantially improves LoRA's performance, effectively narrowing the gap with full fine-tuning. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/mrflogs/LoRA-Pro.
items.title: LoRA-Pro: Are Low-Rank Adapters Properly Optimized?

items.summary: As the scale of large pre-trained models continues to grow, fine-tuning them under limited memory budgets has become increasingly challenging. Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), currently one of the most widely adopted parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods, mitigates this challenge by optimizing only low-rank adaptation matrices, thereby greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters. With the parameter overhead substantially reduced, the activations retained for backpropagation have emerged as the primary remaining memory bottleneck during LoRA fine-tuning. To address this, we propose CARE-LoRA, a data-aware Compressed Activation REconstruction framework. By exploiting the inherent projection structure of LoRA, CARE-LoRA replaces the full input activation with the low-rank compressed activation naturally produced by the LoRA branch. It further computes a lightweight reconstruction matrix during the forward pass with negligible additional computation cost, which is used during backpropagation to reconstruct the gradient signal, thereby keeping LoRA matrices fully trainable. Extensive experiments across diverse models and downstream tasks demonstrate that, while substantially reducing the overall memory footprint, CARE-LoRA achieves competitive or even superior performance compared with standard LoRA and representative LoRA variants. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/fishandyu/CARE-LoRA .
items.title: CARE-LoRA: Compressed Activation REconstruction for Memory-Efficient LoRA

items.summary: We study quality-latency-resource trade-offs in a documentation-grounded retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that uses Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) of the generator. We build a manually verified benchmark of 5,144 question-answer pairs over the official Kubernetes documentation and combine it with a fixed hybrid-retrieval pipeline (BGE-M3 dense, BGE-M3 native sparse, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, cross-encoder reranking). Over this benchmark we ablate 20 LoRA configurations on Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct across rank and target-module choices, and evaluate each on token-level F1, LLM-judged groundedness and correctness (pass@4), inference latency, inference memory, and training cost, all reported with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals. Pareto analysis shows that LoRA adapters acting only on the q and v attention projections consistently dominate the front, while the 3B/8B choice mainly defines operating regime. A param-matched control comparison further indicates that the q/v advantage is structural rather than purely parametric. The benchmark, selected adapters, and code are available at https://github.com/EugPal/rag-lora-tradeoffs.
items.title: Analyzing Quality-Latency-Resource Trade-offs in a Technical Documentation RAG Assistant Using LoRA Adaptation

items.summary: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods have gained considerable popularity for adapting large-scale models to downstream tasks, particularly LoRA and its variants. Existing methods perform low-rank adaptation over the full parameter space. However, fine-tuning within a subspace can achieve comparable effectiveness. Inspired by the observation that pre-trained models possess non-trivial null spaces, we propose Null-space based Low-Rank Adaptation (Null-LoRA). Null-LoRA effectively reduces redundancy and enhances effective rank by freezing portions of the low-rank matrices. To further improve parameter efficiency, Null-LoRA constrains the entire incremental update within the null space, maximizing the utilization of incremental updates to adapt to new task paradigms. Null-LoRA surpasses the state of the art with fewer parameters in extensive experiments across image-text retrieval and visual question answering tasks.
items.title: Null-LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation on Null Space

metadata.query_text: LoRA low-rank adaptation BERT intent classification

items.summary: Foundation models (FMs) achieve strong performance across diverse tasks with task-specific fine-tuning, yet full parameter fine-tuning is often computationally prohibitive for large models. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) reduce this cost by introducing low-rank matrices for tuning fewer parameters. While LoRA allows for efficient fine-tuning, it requires significant data for adaptation, making Federated Learning (FL) an appealing solution due to its privacy-preserving collaborative framework. However, combining LoRA with FL introduces two key challenges: the \textbf{Server-Side Aggregation Bias}, where server-side averaging of LoRA matrices diverges from the ideal global update, and the \textbf{Client-Side Initialization Lag}, emphasizing the need for consistent initialization across rounds. Existing approaches address these challenges individually, limiting their effectiveness. We propose LoRA-FAIR, a novel method that tackles both issues by introducing a correction term on the server, enhancing aggregation efficiency and accuracy. LoRA-FAIR maintains computational and communication efficiency, yielding superior performance over state-of-the-art methods. Experimental results on ViT and MLP-Mixer models across large-scale datasets demonstrate that LoRA-FAIR consistently achieves performance improvements in FL settings.
items.title: LoRA-FAIR: Federated LoRA Fine-Tuning with Aggregation and Initialization Refinement

items.summary: This study introduces a novel approach to example selection in few-shot learning scenarios for dialog intent classification, leveraging logistic regression to refine the set of examples retrieved through traditional similarity-based methods. We evaluate our method on three benchmark datasets: BANKING77, CLINC150, and HWU64, using 5-shot and 10-shot learning setups with 20 demonstrations. Our results show improvements in classification accuracy compared to baseline similarity-based retrieval methods, particularly for semantically similar intents. Notably, we observe a reduction in misclassifications within similar domains after applying our proposed approach. This work contributes to the growing body of research on efficient few-shot learning techniques for natural language understanding tasks, offering insights into enhancing performance in challenging, domain-specific scenarios.
items.title: Logistic Regression-Based Example Selection for Enhanced Few-Shot Learning in Intent Classification

items.summary: To address the challenges posed by dense domain-specific terminology, prominent colloquial expressions, and occasional user input errors in the fault-diagnosis question-answering system for aviation processing modules, this paper proposes an AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN intent recognition (IR) method. Specifically, the BERT model is first employed to extract features and capture the semantic representations of the input queries. Meanwhile, a TextCNN enhanced with Attentive Pooling with Learnable Norms (APLN) is incorporated to effectively capture local textual features while alleviating the information loss that often occurs in traditional max pooling layers. To prevent classification bias caused by perturbations in user input, an adaptive fast gradient method (AFGM) is further applied for adversarial training to improve the model's robustness. Experimental results show that the proposed approach improves the F1 score on the self-constructed dataset by 0.95% compared with the baseline BERT model. To further validate the model's generalizability, comparative experiments on the public Coggle and SMP2017 datasets demonstrate F1-score improvements of 0.71% and 0.59% over BERT.
items.title: Intent Recognition Method for Aircraft Processing Module Fault Diagnosis Based on AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN

items.summary: Given the rapid growth of machine learning publications on platforms such as arXiv, there is a need for systematic approaches to understand their objectives and contributions. This study aimed to analyze scientific intentions across domains, identify research trends, and evaluate the impact of external contextual enrichment on automatic intent classification. We perform a cross-domain comparison of research objectives, methodological designs, and application scenarios in machine learning publications, focusing on computer science and biology. We propose IntentBERT-Wiki, an enhanced BERT model enriched with contextual knowledge from Wikipedia, designed for intent classification in scientific documents. Our dataset comprises annotated sentences extracted from arXiv articles, categorized according to established rhetorical role taxonomies. The model’s performance is evaluated using standard classification metrics and compared to a baseline BERT model. Experimental results show that IntentBERT-Wiki achieves F1-scores of 95.9% in computer science and 87.4% in biology, with corresponding accuracies of 96.5% and 91.4%, outperforming the baseline. These findings demonstrate that Wikipedia-based contextual enrichment can significantly improve intent classification accuracy, enhance the organization of academic discourse, and facilitate cross-domain knowledge transfers. This study contributes to the understanding of how machine learning research is framed across disciplines and provides a scalable framework for scientific content analysis.
items.title: Interdisciplinary Analysis of Machine Learning Applications: Focus on Intent Classification

metadata.query_text: CLINC150 10-shot few-shot intent classification evaluation

items.summary: Intent detection is one of the core components of goal-oriented dialog systems, and detecting out-of-scope (OOS) intents is also a practically important skill. Few-shot learning is attracting much attention to mitigate data scarcity, but OOS detection becomes even more challenging. In this paper, we present a simple yet effective approach, discriminative nearest neighbor classification with deep self-attention. Unlike softmax classifiers, we leverage BERT-style pairwise encoding to train a binary classifier that estimates the best matched training example for a user input. We propose to boost the discriminative ability by transferring a natural language inference (NLI) model. Our extensive experiments on a large-scale multi-domain intent detection task show that our method achieves more stable and accurate in-domain and OOS detection accuracy than RoBERTa-based classifiers and embedding-based nearest neighbor approaches. More notably, the NLI transfer enables our 10-shot model to perform competitively with 50-shot or even full-shot classifiers, while we can keep the inference time constant by leveraging a faster embedding retrieval model.
items.title: Discriminative Nearest Neighbor Few-Shot Intent Detection by Transferring Natural Language Inference

risks: LoRA 在 BERT 上用于 CLINC150 10-shot 意图分类的有效性与效率尚未被公开验证（G001、G003）。

LoRA 在 BERT 上的泛化性可能受限于意图分类的领域特性，导致跨类别或跨领域性能下降（G005）。
plan.scope: 以 BERT（基线）为基础模型，以 LoRA 为参数高效适配方法，在 CLINC150 数据集上进行每类 10-shot 意图分类实验。评估 LoRA 在 BERT 上的适配效果、效率与稳定性，并与基线 BERT 进行对比。

Analyzing Quality-Latency-Resource Trade-offs in a Technical Documentation RAG Assistant Using LoRA Adaptation.
We study quality-latency-resource trade-offs in a documentation-grounded retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that uses Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) of the generator.

We build a manually verified benchmark of 5,144 question-answer pairs over the official Kubernetes documentation and combine it with a fixed hybrid-retrieval pipeline (BGE-M3 dense, BGE-M3 native sparse, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, cross-encoder reranking).

Null-LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation on Null Space.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods have gained considerable popularity for adapting large-scale models to downstream tasks, particularly LoRA and its variants.
Existing methods perform low-rank adaptation over the full parameter space.
LoRA-FAIR: Federated LoRA Fine-Tuning with Aggregation and Initialization Refinement.
Foundation models (FMs) achieve strong performance across diverse tasks with task-specific fine-tuning, yet full parameter fine-tuning is often computationally prohibitive for large models.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) reduce this cost by introducing low-rank matrices for tuning fewer parameters.
Logistic Regression-Based Example Selection for Enhanced Few-Shot Learning in Intent Classification.

This study introduces a novel approach to example selection in few-shot learning scenarios for dialog intent classification, leveraging logistic regression to refine the set of examples retrieved through traditional similarity-based methods.
We evaluate our method on three benchmark datasets: BANKING77, CLINC150, and HWU64, using 5-shot and 10-shot learning setups with 20 demonstrations.
CLINC150.

Intent Recognition Method for Aircraft Processing Module Fault Diagnosis Based on AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN.

To address the challenges posed by dense domain-specific terminology, prominent colloquial expressions, and occasional user input errors in the fault-diagnosis question-answering system for aviation processing modules, this paper proposes an AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN intent recognition (IR) method.
Specifically, the BERT model is first employed to extract features and capture the semantic representations of the input queries.

Interdisciplinary Analysis of Machine Learning Applications: Focus on Intent Classification.
Given the rapid growth of machine learning publications on platforms such as arXiv, there is a need for systematic approaches to understand their objectives and contributions.
This study aimed to analyze scientific intentions across domains, identify research trends, and evaluate the impact of external contextual enrichment on automatic intent classification.

Discriminative Nearest Neighbor Few-Shot Intent Detection by Transferring Natural Language Inference.
Intent detection is one of the core components of goal-oriented dialog systems, and detecting out-of-scope (OOS) intents is also a practically important skill.
Few-shot learning is attracting much attention to mitigate data scarcity, but OOS detection becomes even more challenging.

metadata.query_text: "BERT Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding"

raw_candidates.snippet: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers. As a result, the pre-trained BERT model can be fine-tuned with just one additional output layer to create state-of-the-art models for a wide range of tasks, such as question answering and language inference, without substantial task-specific architecture modifications. BERT is conceptually simple and empirically powerful. It obtains new state-of-the-art results on eleven natural language processing tasks, including pushing the GLUE score to 80.5% (7.7% point absolute improvement), MultiNLI accuracy to 86.7% (4.6% absolute improvement), SQuAD v1.1 question answering Test F1 to 93.2 (1.5 point absolute improvement) and SQuAD v2.0 Test F1 to 83.1 (5.1 point absolute improvement).
raw_candidates.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

metadata.query_text: "LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models"

raw_candidates.snippet: An important paradigm of natural language processing consists of large-scale pre-training on general domain data and adaptation to particular tasks or domains. As we pre-train larger models, full fine-tuning, which retrains all model parameters, becomes less feasible. Using GPT-3 175B as an example -- deploying independent instances of fine-tuned models, each with 175B parameters, is prohibitively expensive. We propose Low-Rank Adaptation, or LoRA, which freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks. Compared to GPT-3 175B fine-tuned with Adam, LoRA can reduce the number of trainable parameters by 10,000 times and the GPU memory requirement by 3 times. LoRA performs on-par or better than fine-tuning in model quality on RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3, despite having fewer trainable parameters, a higher training throughput, and, unlike adapters, no additional inference latency. We also provide an empirical investigation into rank-deficiency in language model adaptation, which sheds light on the efficacy of LoRA. We release a package that facilitates the integration of LoRA with PyTorch models and provide our implementations and model checkpoints for RoBERTa, DeBERTa, and GPT-2 at https://github.com/microsoft/LoRA.
raw_candidates.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
