metadata.query_text: LoRA low-rank adaptation BERT intent classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Low-rank adaptation, also known as LoRA, has emerged as a prominent method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of foundation models. Despite its computational efficiency, LoRA still yields inferior performance compared to full fine-tuning. In this paper, we first uncover a fundamental connection between the optimization processes of LoRA and full fine-tuning: using LoRA for optimization is mathematically equivalent to full fine-tuning using a low-rank gradient for parameter updates. And this low-rank gradient can be expressed in terms of the gradients of the two low-rank matrices in LoRA. Leveraging this insight, we introduce LoRA-Pro, a method that enhances LoRA's performance by strategically adjusting the gradients of these low-rank matrices. This adjustment allows the low-rank gradient to more accurately approximate the full fine-tuning gradient, thereby narrowing the performance gap between LoRA and full fine-tuning. Furthermore, we theoretically derive the optimal solutions for adjusting the gradients of the low-rank matrices, applying them during fine-tuning in LoRA-Pro. We conduct extensive experiments across natural language understanding, dialogue generation, mathematical reasoning, code generation, and image classification tasks, demonstrating that LoRA-Pro substantially improves LoRA's performance, effectively narrowing the gap with full fine-tuning. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/mrflogs/LoRA-Pro.
raw_candidates.title: LoRA-Pro: Are Low-Rank Adapters Properly Optimized?

metadata.query_text: LoRA low-rank adaptation BERT intent classification

raw_candidates.snippet: As the scale of large pre-trained models continues to grow, fine-tuning them under limited memory budgets has become increasingly challenging. Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), currently one of the most widely adopted parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods, mitigates this challenge by optimizing only low-rank adaptation matrices, thereby greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters. With the parameter overhead substantially reduced, the activations retained for backpropagation have emerged as the primary remaining memory bottleneck during LoRA fine-tuning. To address this, we propose CARE-LoRA, a data-aware Compressed Activation REconstruction framework. By exploiting the inherent projection structure of LoRA, CARE-LoRA replaces the full input activation with the low-rank compressed activation naturally produced by the LoRA branch. It further computes a lightweight reconstruction matrix during the forward pass with negligible additional computation cost, which is used during backpropagation to reconstruct the gradient signal, thereby keeping LoRA matrices fully trainable. Extensive experiments across diverse models and downstream tasks demonstrate that, while substantially reducing the overall memory footprint, CARE-LoRA achieves competitive or even superior performance compared with standard LoRA and representative LoRA variants. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/fishandyu/CARE-LoRA .
raw_candidates.title: CARE-LoRA: Compressed Activation REconstruction for Memory-Efficient LoRA

raw_candidates.snippet: We study quality-latency-resource trade-offs in a documentation-grounded retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system that uses Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) of the generator. We build a manually verified benchmark of 5,144 question-answer pairs over the official Kubernetes documentation and combine it with a fixed hybrid-retrieval pipeline (BGE-M3 dense, BGE-M3 native sparse, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, cross-encoder reranking). Over this benchmark we ablate 20 LoRA configurations on Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct across rank and target-module choices, and evaluate each on token-level F1, LLM-judged groundedness and correctness (pass@4), inference latency, inference memory, and training cost, all reported with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals. Pareto analysis shows that LoRA adapters acting only on the q and v attention projections consistently dominate the front, while the 3B/8B choice mainly defines operating regime. A param-matched control comparison further indicates that the q/v advantage is structural rather than purely parametric. The benchmark, selected adapters, and code are available at https://github.com/EugPal/rag-lora-tradeoffs.
raw_candidates.title: Analyzing Quality-Latency-Resource Trade-offs in a Technical Documentation RAG Assistant Using LoRA Adaptation

raw_candidates.snippet: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods have gained considerable popularity for adapting large-scale models to downstream tasks, particularly LoRA and its variants. Existing methods perform low-rank adaptation over the full parameter space. However, fine-tuning within a subspace can achieve comparable effectiveness. Inspired by the observation that pre-trained models possess non-trivial null spaces, we propose Null-space based Low-Rank Adaptation (Null-LoRA). Null-LoRA effectively reduces redundancy and enhances effective rank by freezing portions of the low-rank matrices. To further improve parameter efficiency, Null-LoRA constrains the entire incremental update within the null space, maximizing the utilization of incremental updates to adapt to new task paradigms. Null-LoRA surpasses the state of the art with fewer parameters in extensive experiments across image-text retrieval and visual question answering tasks.
raw_candidates.title: Null-LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation on Null Space

metadata.query_text: LoRA low-rank adaptation BERT intent classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Foundation models (FMs) achieve strong performance across diverse tasks with task-specific fine-tuning, yet full parameter fine-tuning is often computationally prohibitive for large models. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) reduce this cost by introducing low-rank matrices for tuning fewer parameters. While LoRA allows for efficient fine-tuning, it requires significant data for adaptation, making Federated Learning (FL) an appealing solution due to its privacy-preserving collaborative framework. However, combining LoRA with FL introduces two key challenges: the \textbf{Server-Side Aggregation Bias}, where server-side averaging of LoRA matrices diverges from the ideal global update, and the \textbf{Client-Side Initialization Lag}, emphasizing the need for consistent initialization across rounds. Existing approaches address these challenges individually, limiting their effectiveness. We propose LoRA-FAIR, a novel method that tackles both issues by introducing a correction term on the server, enhancing aggregation efficiency and accuracy. LoRA-FAIR maintains computational and communication efficiency, yielding superior performance over state-of-the-art methods. Experimental results on ViT and MLP-Mixer models across large-scale datasets demonstrate that LoRA-FAIR consistently achieves performance improvements in FL settings.
raw_candidates.title: LoRA-FAIR: Federated LoRA Fine-Tuning with Aggregation and Initialization Refinement

raw_candidates.snippet: This study introduces a novel approach to example selection in few-shot learning scenarios for dialog intent classification, leveraging logistic regression to refine the set of examples retrieved through traditional similarity-based methods. We evaluate our method on three benchmark datasets: BANKING77, CLINC150, and HWU64, using 5-shot and 10-shot learning setups with 20 demonstrations. Our results show improvements in classification accuracy compared to baseline similarity-based retrieval methods, particularly for semantically similar intents. Notably, we observe a reduction in misclassifications within similar domains after applying our proposed approach. This work contributes to the growing body of research on efficient few-shot learning techniques for natural language understanding tasks, offering insights into enhancing performance in challenging, domain-specific scenarios.
raw_candidates.title: Logistic Regression-Based Example Selection for Enhanced Few-Shot Learning in Intent Classification

raw_candidates.snippet: To address the challenges posed by dense domain-specific terminology, prominent colloquial expressions, and occasional user input errors in the fault-diagnosis question-answering system for aviation processing modules, this paper proposes an AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN intent recognition (IR) method. Specifically, the BERT model is first employed to extract features and capture the semantic representations of the input queries. Meanwhile, a TextCNN enhanced with Attentive Pooling with Learnable Norms (APLN) is incorporated to effectively capture local textual features while alleviating the information loss that often occurs in traditional max pooling layers. To prevent classification bias caused by perturbations in user input, an adaptive fast gradient method (AFGM) is further applied for adversarial training to improve the model's robustness. Experimental results show that the proposed approach improves the F1 score on the self-constructed dataset by 0.95% compared with the baseline BERT model. To further validate the model's generalizability, comparative experiments on the public Coggle and SMP2017 datasets demonstrate F1-score improvements of 0.71% and 0.59% over BERT.
raw_candidates.title: Intent Recognition Method for Aircraft Processing Module Fault Diagnosis Based on AFGM-BERT-TCAPLN

raw_candidates.snippet: Given the rapid growth of machine learning publications on platforms such as arXiv, there is a need for systematic approaches to understand their objectives and contributions. This study aimed to analyze scientific intentions across domains, identify research trends, and evaluate the impact of external contextual enrichment on automatic intent classification. We perform a cross-domain comparison of research objectives, methodological designs, and application scenarios in machine learning publications, focusing on computer science and biology. We propose IntentBERT-Wiki, an enhanced BERT model enriched with contextual knowledge from Wikipedia, designed for intent classification in scientific documents. Our dataset comprises annotated sentences extracted from arXiv articles, categorized according to established rhetorical role taxonomies. The model’s performance is evaluated using standard classification metrics and compared to a baseline BERT model. Experimental results show that IntentBERT-Wiki achieves F1-scores of 95.9% in computer science and 87.4% in biology, with corresponding accuracies of 96.5% and 91.4%, outperforming the baseline. These findings demonstrate that Wikipedia-based contextual enrichment can significantly improve intent classification accuracy, enhance the organization of academic discourse, and facilitate cross-domain knowledge transfers. This study contributes to the understanding of how machine learning research is framed across disciplines and provides a scalable framework for scientific content analysis.
raw_candidates.title: Interdisciplinary Analysis of Machine Learning Applications: Focus on Intent Classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Intent detection is one of the core components of goal-oriented dialog systems, and detecting out-of-scope (OOS) intents is also a practically important skill. Few-shot learning is attracting much attention to mitigate data scarcity, but OOS detection becomes even more challenging. In this paper, we present a simple yet effective approach, discriminative nearest neighbor classification with deep self-attention. Unlike softmax classifiers, we leverage BERT-style pairwise encoding to train a binary classifier that estimates the best matched training example for a user input. We propose to boost the discriminative ability by transferring a natural language inference (NLI) model. Our extensive experiments on a large-scale multi-domain intent detection task show that our method achieves more stable and accurate in-domain and OOS detection accuracy than RoBERTa-based classifiers and embedding-based nearest neighbor approaches. More notably, the NLI transfer enables our 10-shot model to perform competitively with 50-shot or even full-shot classifiers, while we can keep the inference time constant by leveraging a faster embedding retrieval model.
raw_candidates.title: Discriminative Nearest Neighbor Few-Shot Intent Detection by Transferring Natural Language Inference

metadata.query_text: skin lesion multi-class classification small sample imbalanced dermatoscopy

items.summary: Deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) models have been widely explored for skin disease diagnosis and some of them have achieved the diagnostic outcomes comparable or even superior to those of dermatologists. However, broad implementation of DCNN in skin disease detection is hindered by small size and data imbalance of the publically accessible skin lesion datasets. This paper proposes a novel single-model based strategy for classification of skin lesions on small and imbalanced datasets. First, various DCNNs are trained on different small and imbalanced datasets to verify that the models with moderate complexity outperform the larger models. Second, regularization DropOut and DropBlock are added to reduce overfitting and a Modified RandAugment augmentation strategy is proposed to deal with the defects of sample underrepresentation in the small dataset. Finally, a novel Multi-Weighted New Loss (MWNL) function and an end-to-end cumulative learning strategy (CLS) are introduced to overcome the challenge of uneven sample size and classification difficulty and to reduce the impact of abnormal samples on training. By combining Modified RandAugment, MWNL and CLS, our single DCNN model method achieved the classification accuracy comparable or superior to those of multiple ensembling models on different dermoscopic image datasets. Our study shows that this method is able to achieve a high classification performance at a low cost of computational resources and inference time, potentially suitable to implement in mobile devices for automated screening of skin lesions and many other malignancies in low resource settings.
items.title: Single Model Deep Learning on Imbalanced Small Datasets for Skin Lesion Classification

metadata.query_text: transfer learning skin lesion classification imbalanced data ISIC

items.summary: Accurate skin lesion classification can benefit from lesion segmentation masks, but requiring masks or an auxiliary segmentation model during inference reduces clinical practicality and increases computational complexity. This work introduces Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation (PLCRD), a teacher-student framework that exploits lesion masks exclusively during training while preserving image-only inference. The privileged teacher jointly analyzes the original dermoscopic image and its mask-guided lesion region to learn lesion-specific and contextual diagnostic representations. An image-only student is then trained through complementary knowledge-transfer mechanisms that convey the teacher's diagnostic distribution, lesion-focused attention, inter-lesion relational geometry, and lesion-context structure. PLCRD decomposes deep representations into lesion and contextual embeddings and transfers their relational organization through inter-lesion similarity alignment, lesion-context affinity matching, separation regularization, and class-aware relational learning. This formulation avoids direct feature matching between heterogeneous teacher and student architectures and enables the student to internalize mask-informed diagnostic structure without accessing masks at deployment. The framework was evaluated on HAM10000 using lesion-disjoint data partitioning and externally validated on ISIC 2018 without retraining. PLCRD achieved a lesion-level macro-F1 of 0.773 +/- 0.018, balanced accuracy of 0.764 +/- 0.023, and macro-AUROC of 0.976 +/- 0.002 on HAM10000, together with a macro-F1 of 0.732 +/- 0.008 on ISIC 2018. The results indicate that privileged lesion annotations can be transformed into transferable relational knowledge, yielding a practical and interpretable approach to mask-free skin lesion classification.
items.title: Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation for Mask-Free Skin Lesion Classification

items.summary: Background/Objectives: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have demonstrated impressive performance in dealing with large-scale natural image datasets. They have started to be used in medical image classification problems as well. However, how they behave under real-world conditions, such as data scarcity and extreme class imbalance, has not been well investigated. In this study, we examine the feasibility of using a standard Vision Transformer Base model that learned from scratch how to classify skin lesion images into multiple classes using the ISIC 2019 dataset. Methods: The Vision Transformer architecture was trained from scratch using stratified splitting of the data, class-balanced cross-entropy loss, multi-seed initialization, and control of hyperparameters such as patch size and dropout rate. The evaluation of the Vision Transformer architecture was performed using a hold-out test set with metrics such as accuracy, macro-F1, weighted-F1, and analysis of the confusion matrix. Results: Across all configurations, the training exhibited substantial instability and consistent overfitting behavior, with an average accuracy gap between validation and test sets of 22.7%. Test accuracy ranged from 8.0% to 37.8%, showing high sensitivity to initialization. For minority classes, the F1-score remained very low (F1 < 0.05) even though the classes were balanced in the loss function. Conclusions: The results indicate that a standard ViT-Base model trained from scratch can exhibit pronounced instability and a tendency toward majority-class bias when applied to multi-class skin lesion classification under conditions of extreme class imbalance and data scarcity. The findings point to the limitations of using simple transformer models without pre-training or other forms of inductive bias in scarce data settings.
items.title: Systematic Failure of Vision Transformers in Imbalanced Skin Lesion Classification

metadata.query_text: ISIC skin lesion classification sample distribution imbalanced classes

items.summary: State-of-the-art methods primarily use dermoscopic skin images to classify lesions. Incorporating clinical metadata to improve melanoma diagnosis has increased popularity in the community. This study explores whether a single modality (dermoscopic images) or multimodality (dermoscopic images and clinical metadata) enhances performance for skin lesion classification. In particular, it tries to answer the question by comparing three multimodal architectures. This paper introduces the ISIC-DICM-17K dataset, a curated balanced dataset comprising 17,060 dermoscopic images and clinical metadata with an equal distribution of melanoma and non-melanoma classes. We evaluate the impact of including clinical metadata on multimodal model performance using both supervised and transfer learning models. Our results show that including metadata significantly enhances supervised learning models' performance, while all transfer learning models outperform supervised learning models with 3 % to 12 % performance improvement. Our statistical and visual analyses highlight the importance of clinical metadata in improving feature clustering and class separability, and the HeatMap ${ }_{\text {index }}$ analysis shows the effectiveness in identifying relevant features on lesion images. Our GitHub repository (accessible at https://github.com/mmu-dermatology-research/isic-dicm-17k) contains the ISIC-DICM-17K dataset and image IDs referencing the original ISIC dataset sources.
items.title: Skin Lesion Classification using Dermoscopic Images and Clinical Metadata: Insights from Multimodal Models

items.summary: Skin cancer, including the highly lethal malignant melanoma, poses a significant global health challenge with a rising incidence rate. Early detection plays a pivotal role in improving survival rates. This study aims to develop an advanced deep learning-based approach for accurate skin lesion classification, addressing challenges such as limited data availability, class imbalance, and noise. Modern deep neural network designs, such as ResNeXt101, SeResNeXt101, ResNet152V2, DenseNet201, GoogLeNet, and Xception, which are used in the study and ze optimised using the SGD technique. The dataset comprises diverse skin lesion images from the HAM10000 and ISIC datasets. Noise and artifacts are tackled using image inpainting, and data augmentation techniques enhance training sample diversity. The ensemble technique is utilized, creating both average and weighted average ensemble models. Grid search optimizes model weight distribution. The individual models exhibit varying performance, with metrics including recall, precision, F1 score, and MCC. The "Average ensemble model" achieves harmonious balance, emphasizing precision, F1 score, and recall, yielding high performance. The "Weighted ensemble model" capitalizes on individual models' strengths, showcasing heightened precision and MCC, yielding outstanding performance. The ensemble models consistently outperform individual models, with the average ensemble model attaining a macro-average ROC-AUC score of 96 % and the weighted ensemble model achieving a macro-average ROC-AUC score of 97 %. This research demonstrates the efficacy of ensemble techniques in significantly improving skin lesion classification accuracy. By harnessing the strengths of individual models and addressing their limitations, the ensemble models exhibit robust and reliable performance across various metrics. The findings underscore the potential of ensemble techniques in enhancing medical diagnostics and contributing to improved patient outcomes in skin lesion diagnosis.
items.title: Enhancing skin lesion classification with advanced deep learning ensemble models: a path towards accurate medical diagnostics.

ablations.expected_observation: recover the unchanged frozen baseline path
ablations.name: module-off parity

ablations.change: enable only explainable_privileged_lesion_context_distillation under the matched budget
ablations.expected_observation: 通过病灶注意力一致性约束，学生模型能够在缺乏掩码输入的情况下保持对病灶区域的关注，从而提升对罕见病变亚型的识别能力；同时，可解释性审计输出（注意力图与关系图）能够为临床医生提供可操作的决策支持，缩小模型决策与专家判断的差距。
ablations.name: single-module causal test

baseline.description: Accepted evidence constrains baseline selection; implementation version, reproduction, and deployment fit remain pilot gates.
baseline.name: Skin Lesion Segmentation and Classification for ISIC 2018 Using Traditional Classifiers with Hand-Crafted Features

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 皮肤镜图像数据集存在严重类别不均衡（最小类别样本数<50张）且总样本规模<1000张/类，同时临床上需要可解释的决策支持以辅助诊断。, 在学生模型的关系蒸馏损失中引入"病灶注意力一致性约束"（Lesion Attention Consistency Constraint, LACC），该约束通过最小化学生模型与教师模型在病灶区域的注意力分布差异，确保学生模型在缺乏掩码输入的情况下仍能保持对病灶区域的关注度，从而提升模型对罕见病变亚型的识别能力与可解释性。 should 预期在HAM10000与ISIC数据集上，EPLCRD相较于基线（仅使用图像输入的学生模型）在罕见病变亚型的宏观F1分数提升5–8%，同时病灶注意力图与类间关系图的可解释性审计准确率达到85%以上。 because 教师模型通过联合分析原始皮肤镜图像与病灶分割掩码，学习病灶特异性与上下文诊断表示；学生模型仅接收图像输入，通过关系蒸馏损失（包括病灶注意力一致性损失、类间关系对齐损失与类内关系正则化）内化教师模型的诊断结构。可解释性审计通过可视化学生模型的病灶注意力图与类间关系图实现，确保决策路径与临床专家的诊断逻辑一致。 addresses EPLCRD依赖于病灶分割掩码作为特权信息，在实际部署时需确保掩码生成的可靠性与一致性；此外，关系蒸馏机制对教师模型的病灶-上下文结构解耦能力存在上限，可能无法完全覆盖所有罕见病变亚型。, while 设置病灶注意力一致性约束的阈值（如KL散度<0.1）作为早停条件；若学生模型的注意力分布与教师模型偏离超过阈值，则触发重新蒸馏或数据增强策略调整。此外，对罕见病变亚型的预测结果进行人工审核，确保临床安全。.

integration_contracts.input: 学生模型输入为单张皮肤镜图像（RGB，分辨率224x224或256x256）；教师模型输入为原始图像与对应的病灶分割掩码（二值图，与图像同尺寸）。
integration_contracts.output: 学生模型输出为类别概率分布（7分类或更多）与病灶注意力图（热力图，与输入图像同尺寸）；同时生成类间关系图（可视化不同病变亚型间的特征相似度与差异）。

baseline.environment: not yet frozen; record hardware, framework, precision, export path, and dependency lock
baseline.name: Skin Lesion Segmentation and Classification for ISIC 2018 Using Traditional Classifiers with Hand-Crafted Features
baseline.seed_policy: three fixed seeds (1, 2, 3) for all pilot comparisons
baseline.split: not yet frozen; preserve the documented benchmark split and record data hashes
baseline.version_or_commit: inferred from evidence relation at arxiv:1807.07001; reproduce and freeze an implementation before module integration

limitations: Limited generalizability of findings to highly heterogeneous clinical settings due to reliance on specific datasets (e.g., ISIC, HAM10000).
Lack of standardized benchmarks for evaluating model robustness across diverse small and imbalanced datasets.
Performance gains from multimodal approaches may not generalize to settings where clinical metadata is unavailable or noisy.
Class imbalance mitigation strategies often require hyperparameter tuning, which may not be feasible in resource-constrained environments.
Interpretability methods (e.g., attention maps) may not provide sufficient clinical insight for all lesion types or minority classes.
Trade-offs between model complexity and interpretability are not systematically evaluated across studies.
ISIC and related datasets may not capture the full diversity of skin lesions or clinical conditions, limiting generalizability.
Lack of standardized evaluation protocols for comparing models across different datasets and clinical settings.
supported_claims: Single-model deep learning strategies with regularization (e.g., DropOut, DropBlock) and augmentation (Modified RandAugment) can achieve classification performance comparable to or better than ensemble models on small and imbalanced skin lesion datasets.

hypothesis.condition: 皮肤镜图像数据集存在严重类别不均衡（最小类别样本数<50张）且总样本规模<1000张/类，同时临床上需要可解释的决策支持以辅助诊断。
hypothesis.guardrail: 设置病灶注意力一致性约束的阈值（如KL散度<0.1）作为早停条件；若学生模型的注意力分布与教师模型偏离超过阈值，则触发重新蒸馏或数据增强策略调整。此外，对罕见病变亚型的预测结果进行人工审核，确保临床安全。
hypothesis.intervention: 在学生模型的关系蒸馏损失中引入"病灶注意力一致性约束"（Lesion Attention Consistency Constraint, LACC），该约束通过最小化学生模型与教师模型在病灶区域的注意力分布差异，确保学生模型在缺乏掩码输入的情况下仍能保持对病灶区域的关注度，从而提升模型对罕见病变亚型的识别能力与可解释性。
hypothesis.limitation: EPLCRD依赖于病灶分割掩码作为特权信息，在实际部署时需确保掩码生成的可靠性与一致性；此外，关系蒸馏机制对教师模型的病灶-上下文结构解耦能力存在上限，可能无法完全覆盖所有罕见病变亚型。
hypothesis.mechanism: 教师模型通过联合分析原始皮肤镜图像与病灶分割掩码，学习病灶特异性与上下文诊断表示；学生模型仅接收图像输入，通过关系蒸馏损失（包括病灶注意力一致性损失、类间关系对齐损失与类内关系正则化）内化教师模型的诊断结构。可解释性审计通过可视化学生模型的病灶注意力图与类间关系图实现，确保决策路径与临床专家的诊断逻辑一致。
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在HAM10000与ISIC数据集上，EPLCRD相较于基线（仅使用图像输入的学生模型）在罕见病变亚型的宏观F1分数提升5–8%，同时病灶注意力图与类间关系图的可解释性审计准确率达到85%以上。

assumptions: 皮肤镜病变多分类的类别定义和标注标准可能存在差异，影响方法的可比性。
小样本不均衡场景下，现有方法的泛化能力可能受限，难以直接推广到临床应用。
可解释性审计方法的临床验证不足，可能无法满足实际需求。
数据集的样本分布和质量可能影响方法的有效性，需进一步验证。
未明确目标类别数量和具体病变亚型，可能影响方法选择和评估标准。

the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: 训练阶段：教师模型（基于ResNet50或EfficientNet-B4）与学生模型（基于MobileNetV3或ViT-Tiny）在单张A100 GPU上训练约12–24小时；推理阶段：学生模型单张图像推理时间<50ms，适合移动设备部署。
modules.failure_mode: 若病灶分割掩码质量较差（如掩码覆盖不完整或包含噪声），教师模型的特权信息将被污染，导致学生模型的病灶注意力分布异常，进而影响罕见病变亚型的识别与可解释性审计的准确性。此外，若类间关系对齐损失设置不当，可能导致学生模型过度拟合多数类，忽视罕见病变亚型。
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: 学生模型输入为单张皮肤镜图像（RGB，分辨率224x224或256x256）；教师模型输入为原始图像与对应的病灶分割掩码（二值图，与图像同尺寸）。
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: explainable_privileged_lesion_context_distillation
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: 特权知识蒸馏框架（PLCRD）的学生模型训练模块，用于将教师模型的诊断知识转移至图像输入的学生模型。
modules.output_semantics: 学生模型输出为类别概率分布（7分类或更多）与病灶注意力图（热力图，与输入图像同尺寸）；同时生成类间关系图（可视化不同病变亚型间的特征相似度与差异）。
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: 通过病灶注意力一致性约束，学生模型能够在缺乏掩码输入的情况下保持对病灶区域的关注，从而提升对罕见病变亚型的识别能力；同时，可解释性审计输出（注意力图与关系图）能够为临床医生提供可操作的决策支持，缩小模型决策与专家判断的差距。
modules.proposed_role: 在EPLCRD框架下，该模块被扩展为可解释的关系蒸馏模块，通过嵌入病灶注意力一致性约束与关系图正则化，实现对学生模型决策路径的可解释性审计与罕见病变亚型的识别增强。

constraints: 皮肤镜病变多分类的类别定义和标注标准可能存在差异，影响方法的可比性。
小样本不均衡场景下，现有方法的泛化能力可能受限，难以直接推广到临床应用。

research.intended_claim: 提出一种可解释的特权知识蒸馏框架（Explainable Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation, EPLCRD），在训练阶段引入病灶分割掩码作为特权信息，通过关系蒸馏机制将教师模型的病灶-上下文诊断结构转移至仅使用图像输入的学生模型。核心创新点在于：(1) 将教师模型的注意力图与关系图显式解耦为病灶嵌入与上下文嵌入，并通过类间关系对齐与类内关系正则化约束学生模型的特征组织；(2) 在蒸馏损失中嵌入可解释性审计项（如病灶注意力一致性损失与类间关系可视化约束），确保学生模型的决策路径可追溯；(3) 采用修正的RandAugment与MWNL损失（VF001、VF002）缓解小样本与类别不均衡问题。该方法在保持推理时仅需图像输入的同时，实现对教师模型临床可解释性知识的继承。
research.observed_problem: EPLCRD依赖于病灶分割掩码作为特权信息，在实际部署时需确保掩码生成的可靠性与一致性；此外，关系蒸馏机制对教师模型的病灶-上下文结构解耦能力存在上限，可能无法完全覆盖所有罕见病变亚型。
research.proposed_mechanism: 教师模型通过联合分析原始皮肤镜图像与病灶分割掩码，学习病灶特异性与上下文诊断表示；学生模型仅接收图像输入，通过关系蒸馏损失（包括病灶注意力一致性损失、类间关系对齐损失与类内关系正则化）内化教师模型的诊断结构。可解释性审计通过可视化学生模型的病灶注意力图与类间关系图实现，确保决策路径与临床专家的诊断逻辑一致。
research.scientific_setting: 皮肤镜图像多分类（如良性vs恶性、多种皮肤病变亚型）与可解释性审计，重点关注小样本（<1000张/类）和严重类别不均衡（最小类别样本数<50张）的场景。
research.success_metric: 罕见病变亚型的宏观F1分数（macro-F1）
research.target_problem: 小样本且类别不均衡条件下的皮肤镜病变多分类与可解释性审计

method.problem_method_insight: 在小样本且严重类别不均衡（最小类别样本数<50张）的皮肤镜病变多分类场景中，现有方法（如单模型策略、集成学习或特权知识蒸馏）在可解释性审计与临床决策支持方面存在显著不足。具体表现为：(1) 标准Vision Transformer从头训练时表现不稳定且存在多数类偏见（VF005）；(2) 即使采用集成学习（VF006）或特权知识蒸馏（VF003），其内部决策逻辑的可解释性仍无法直接映射到临床可操作的审计路径；(3) 类别不均衡问题（VF002）与样本稀缺（VF001）进一步限制了模型的泛化能力与可信度。
risks: 皮肤镜病变多分类的类别定义和标注标准可能存在差异，影响方法的可比性。

metadata.query_text: skin lesion multi-class classification small sample imbalanced dermatoscopy

raw_candidates.snippet: Deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) models have been widely explored for skin disease diagnosis and some of them have achieved the diagnostic outcomes comparable or even superior to those of dermatologists. However, broad implementation of DCNN in skin disease detection is hindered by small size and data imbalance of the publically accessible skin lesion datasets. This paper proposes a novel single-model based strategy for classification of skin lesions on small and imbalanced datasets. First, various DCNNs are trained on different small and imbalanced datasets to verify that the models with moderate complexity outperform the larger models. Second, regularization DropOut and DropBlock are added to reduce overfitting and a Modified RandAugment augmentation strategy is proposed to deal with the defects of sample underrepresentation in the small dataset. Finally, a novel Multi-Weighted New Loss (MWNL) function and an end-to-end cumulative learning strategy (CLS) are introduced to overcome the challenge of uneven sample size and classification difficulty and to reduce the impact of abnormal samples on training. By combining Modified RandAugment, MWNL and CLS, our single DCNN model method achieved the classification accuracy comparable or superior to those of multiple ensembling models on different dermoscopic image datasets. Our study shows that this method is able to achieve a high classification performance at a low cost of computational resources and inference time, potentially suitable to implement in mobile devices for automated screening of skin lesions and many other malignancies in low resource settings.
raw_candidates.title: Single Model Deep Learning on Imbalanced Small Datasets for Skin Lesion Classification

metadata.query_text: transfer learning skin lesion classification imbalanced data ISIC

raw_candidates.snippet: Accurate skin lesion classification can benefit from lesion segmentation masks, but requiring masks or an auxiliary segmentation model during inference reduces clinical practicality and increases computational complexity. This work introduces Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation (PLCRD), a teacher-student framework that exploits lesion masks exclusively during training while preserving image-only inference. The privileged teacher jointly analyzes the original dermoscopic image and its mask-guided lesion region to learn lesion-specific and contextual diagnostic representations. An image-only student is then trained through complementary knowledge-transfer mechanisms that convey the teacher's diagnostic distribution, lesion-focused attention, inter-lesion relational geometry, and lesion-context structure. PLCRD decomposes deep representations into lesion and contextual embeddings and transfers their relational organization through inter-lesion similarity alignment, lesion-context affinity matching, separation regularization, and class-aware relational learning. This formulation avoids direct feature matching between heterogeneous teacher and student architectures and enables the student to internalize mask-informed diagnostic structure without accessing masks at deployment. The framework was evaluated on HAM10000 using lesion-disjoint data partitioning and externally validated on ISIC 2018 without retraining. PLCRD achieved a lesion-level macro-F1 of 0.773 +/- 0.018, balanced accuracy of 0.764 +/- 0.023, and macro-AUROC of 0.976 +/- 0.002 on HAM10000, together with a macro-F1 of 0.732 +/- 0.008 on ISIC 2018. The results indicate that privileged lesion annotations can be transformed into transferable relational knowledge, yielding a practical and interpretable approach to mask-free skin lesion classification.
raw_candidates.title: Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation for Mask-Free Skin Lesion Classification

raw_candidates.snippet: Background/Objectives: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have demonstrated impressive performance in dealing with large-scale natural image datasets. They have started to be used in medical image classification problems as well. However, how they behave under real-world conditions, such as data scarcity and extreme class imbalance, has not been well investigated. In this study, we examine the feasibility of using a standard Vision Transformer Base model that learned from scratch how to classify skin lesion images into multiple classes using the ISIC 2019 dataset. Methods: The Vision Transformer architecture was trained from scratch using stratified splitting of the data, class-balanced cross-entropy loss, multi-seed initialization, and control of hyperparameters such as patch size and dropout rate. The evaluation of the Vision Transformer architecture was performed using a hold-out test set with metrics such as accuracy, macro-F1, weighted-F1, and analysis of the confusion matrix. Results: Across all configurations, the training exhibited substantial instability and consistent overfitting behavior, with an average accuracy gap between validation and test sets of 22.7%. Test accuracy ranged from 8.0% to 37.8%, showing high sensitivity to initialization. For minority classes, the F1-score remained very low (F1 < 0.05) even though the classes were balanced in the loss function. Conclusions: The results indicate that a standard ViT-Base model trained from scratch can exhibit pronounced instability and a tendency toward majority-class bias when applied to multi-class skin lesion classification under conditions of extreme class imbalance and data scarcity. The findings point to the limitations of using simple transformer models without pre-training or other forms of inductive bias in scarce data settings.
raw_candidates.title: Systematic Failure of Vision Transformers in Imbalanced Skin Lesion Classification

raw_candidates.snippet: State-of-the-art methods primarily use dermoscopic skin images to classify lesions. Incorporating clinical metadata to improve melanoma diagnosis has increased popularity in the community. This study explores whether a single modality (dermoscopic images) or multimodality (dermoscopic images and clinical metadata) enhances performance for skin lesion classification. In particular, it tries to answer the question by comparing three multimodal architectures. This paper introduces the ISIC-DICM-17K dataset, a curated balanced dataset comprising 17,060 dermoscopic images and clinical metadata with an equal distribution of melanoma and non-melanoma classes. We evaluate the impact of including clinical metadata on multimodal model performance using both supervised and transfer learning models. Our results show that including metadata significantly enhances supervised learning models' performance, while all transfer learning models outperform supervised learning models with 3 % to 12 % performance improvement. Our statistical and visual analyses highlight the importance of clinical metadata in improving feature clustering and class separability, and the HeatMap ${ }_{\text {index }}$ analysis shows the effectiveness in identifying relevant features on lesion images. Our GitHub repository (accessible at https://github.com/mmu-dermatology-research/isic-dicm-17k) contains the ISIC-DICM-17K dataset and image IDs referencing the original ISIC dataset sources.
raw_candidates.title: Skin Lesion Classification using Dermoscopic Images and Clinical Metadata: Insights from Multimodal Models

raw_candidates.snippet: Skin cancer, including the highly lethal malignant melanoma, poses a significant global health challenge with a rising incidence rate. Early detection plays a pivotal role in improving survival rates. This study aims to develop an advanced deep learning-based approach for accurate skin lesion classification, addressing challenges such as limited data availability, class imbalance, and noise. Modern deep neural network designs, such as ResNeXt101, SeResNeXt101, ResNet152V2, DenseNet201, GoogLeNet, and Xception, which are used in the study and ze optimised using the SGD technique. The dataset comprises diverse skin lesion images from the HAM10000 and ISIC datasets. Noise and artifacts are tackled using image inpainting, and data augmentation techniques enhance training sample diversity. The ensemble technique is utilized, creating both average and weighted average ensemble models. Grid search optimizes model weight distribution. The individual models exhibit varying performance, with metrics including recall, precision, F1 score, and MCC. The "Average ensemble model" achieves harmonious balance, emphasizing precision, F1 score, and recall, yielding high performance. The "Weighted ensemble model" capitalizes on individual models' strengths, showcasing heightened precision and MCC, yielding outstanding performance. The ensemble models consistently outperform individual models, with the average ensemble model attaining a macro-average ROC-AUC score of 96 % and the weighted ensemble model achieving a macro-average ROC-AUC score of 97 %. This research demonstrates the efficacy of ensemble techniques in significantly improving skin lesion classification accuracy. By harnessing the strengths of individual models and addressing their limitations, the ensemble models exhibit robust and reliable performance across various metrics. The findings underscore the potential of ensemble techniques in enhancing medical diagnostics and contributing to improved patient outcomes in skin lesion diagnosis.
raw_candidates.title: Enhancing skin lesion classification with advanced deep learning ensemble models: a path towards accurate medical diagnostics.

gap_assessments.summary: Evidence supports the challenge of small sample size in skin lesion multi-class classification, with studies demonstrating instability and overfitting in models trained from scratch under such conditions. Approaches like single-model strategies with regularization and augmentation, as well as ensemble techniques, are proposed to mitigate these issues.

limitations: Performance gains from multimodal approaches may not generalize to settings where clinical metadata is unavailable or noisy.
Class imbalance mitigation strategies often require hyperparameter tuning, which may not be feasible in resource-constrained environments.
gap_assessments.summary: Class imbalance in skin lesion datasets is a well-documented challenge, with evidence showing that standard models (e.g., Vision Transformers) trained from scratch suffer from majority-class bias and instability. Solutions include class-balanced loss functions, transfer learning, and multimodal approaches leveraging clinical metadata to improve performance.

limitations: Interpretability methods (e.g., attention maps) may not provide sufficient clinical insight for all lesion types or minority classes.
Trade-offs between model complexity and interpretability are not systematically evaluated across studies.
gap_assessments.summary: The need for interpretable and clinically actionable models is supported by evidence showing that ensemble techniques and privileged knowledge distillation can improve diagnostic performance while maintaining practicality. However, interpretability remains a challenge, particularly for complex models like Vision Transformers.

limitations: ISIC and related datasets may not capture the full diversity of skin lesions or clinical conditions, limiting generalizability.
Lack of standardized evaluation protocols for comparing models across different datasets and clinical settings.
gap_assessments.summary: The ISIC dataset and its variants (e.g., ISIC-DICM-17K) are widely used benchmarks for skin lesion classification, but their class imbalance and small sample sizes limit their representativeness of real-world clinical scenarios. Studies highlight the need for balanced datasets and external validation to ensure robustness.

limitations: Generalizability of findings to real-world clinical settings is limited by reliance on specific datasets (e.g., ISIC, HAM10000) and lack of external validation in diverse populations.
Interpretability of advanced models (e.g., Vision Transformers, multimodal architectures) remains insufficient for clinical decision support.
Class imbalance mitigation strategies often require significant computational resources or hyperparameter tuning, which may not be feasible in low-resource settings.
The ISIC dataset and its variants may not fully represent the heterogeneity of skin lesions encountered in clinical practice, limiting the applicability of findings.

verified_findings.text: Single-model deep learning strategies with regularization (e.g., DropOut, DropBlock) and augmentation (Modified RandAugment) can achieve classification performance comparable to or better than ensemble models on small and imbalanced skin lesion datasets.

The Multi-Weighted New Loss (MWNL) function and cumulative learning strategy (CLS) reduce the impact of uneven sample sizes and abnormal samples, improving classification performance on imbalanced datasets.

Privileged Lesion-Context Relational Distillation (PLCRD) enables mask-free skin lesion classification by transferring lesion-specific and contextual diagnostic knowledge from a teacher model trained with lesion masks to an image-only student model.

Including clinical metadata alongside dermoscopic images significantly enhances supervised learning model performance for skin lesion classification, with transfer learning models outperforming supervised learning models by 3% to 12%.

Vision Transformers trained from scratch on small and imbalanced skin lesion datasets exhibit pronounced instability, overfitting, and majority-class bias, with test accuracy ranging from 8.0% to 37.8% and F1-scores < 0.05 for minority classes.

Ensemble techniques (e.g., average and weighted ensemble models) significantly improve skin lesion classification performance, achieving macro-average ROC-AUC scores of 96% and 97% on HAM10000 and ISIC datasets.

metadata.query_text: "Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection" official implementation code repository

items.summary: Current state-of-the-art two-stage detectors generate oriented proposals through time-consuming schemes. This diminishes the detectors' speed, thereby becoming the computational bottleneck in advanced oriented object detection systems. This work proposes an effective and simple oriented object detection framework, termed Oriented R-CNN, which is a general two-stage oriented detector with promising accuracy and efficiency. To be specific, in the first stage, we propose an oriented Region Proposal Network (oriented RPN) that directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner. The second stage is oriented R-CNN head for refining oriented Regions of Interest (oriented RoIs) and recognizing them. Without tricks, oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy on two commonly-used datasets for oriented object detection including DOTA (75.87% mAP) and HRSC2016 (96.50% mAP), while having a speed of 15.1 FPS with the image size of 1024$\times$1024 on a single RTX 2080Ti. We hope our work could inspire rethinking the design of oriented detectors and serve as a baseline for oriented object detection. Code is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.
items.title: Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under Baseline Oriented R-CNN is reproduced with identical training protocol and DOTA split as reported in the accepted evidence., Substitute the oriented RoIAlign + oriented RCNN head module with a lightweight RoI head composed of a depthwise separable conv (kernel 3x3, channels reduced to 128) followed by global average pooling and a single linear layer for classification and regression. should Expected 20–30% reduction in FLOPs and memory bandwidth for the RoI head with <0.5% mAP drop on DOTA compared to the baseline Oriented R-CNN under matched settings. because Depthwise separable convolution reduces spatial and channel-wise computation by factoring standard convolutions into depthwise and pointwise operations, while global average pooling collapses spatial dimensions without learnable parameters, directly cutting FLOPs and memory traffic in the RoI head. addresses Channel compression and pooling may slightly reduce localization precision for extremely small objects (<10 pixels) if the pooling stride is too aggressive., while Maintain the same RoI sampling strategy and loss functions; ensure the compressed head does not alter the RoI coordinate system or anchor generation to preserve compatibility with the oriented RPN outputs..

supported_claims: Oriented R-CNN achieves 75.87% mAP on the DOTA dataset with an image size of 1024×1024 on a single RTX 2080Ti.
Oriented R-CNN operates at 15.1 FPS on a single RTX 2080Ti for DOTA inference with 1024×1024 images.
The Oriented RPN in Oriented R-CNN generates oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner, reducing computational overhead.
The official implementation of Oriented R-CNN is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.

hypothesis.condition: Baseline Oriented R-CNN is reproduced with identical training protocol and DOTA split as reported in the accepted evidence.
hypothesis.guardrail: Maintain the same RoI sampling strategy and loss functions; ensure the compressed head does not alter the RoI coordinate system or anchor generation to preserve compatibility with the oriented RPN outputs.
hypothesis.intervention: Substitute the oriented RoIAlign + oriented RCNN head module with a lightweight RoI head composed of a depthwise separable conv (kernel 3x3, channels reduced to 128) followed by global average pooling and a single linear layer for classification and regression.
hypothesis.limitation: Channel compression and pooling may slightly reduce localization precision for extremely small objects (<10 pixels) if the pooling stride is too aggressive.
hypothesis.mechanism: Depthwise separable convolution reduces spatial and channel-wise computation by factoring standard convolutions into depthwise and pointwise operations, while global average pooling collapses spatial dimensions without learnable parameters, directly cutting FLOPs and memory traffic in the RoI head.
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: Expected 20–30% reduction in FLOPs and memory bandwidth for the RoI head with <0.5% mAP drop on DOTA compared to the baseline Oriented R-CNN under matched settings.

assumptions: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。
现有降低高分辨率推理成本的方法可能主要针对水平框检测（HBB），而非旋转框检测（OBB），适用性存在不确定性。
DOTA 数据集版本（v1.0/v2.0）的选择会影响结果可比性，需在检索时明确版本信息。
高分辨率推理成本与精度的权衡曲线可能依赖于具体硬件平台（如 GPU 型号、内存大小），公开文献中的数值可能不具备直接可比性。
the selected insertion point preserves representation semantics
modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: Low: single depthwise separable conv layer (≈128 channels) + global average pooling + linear layer; minimal parameter increase vs. baseline oriented RCNN head.
modules.failure_mode: Over-aggressive channel reduction or pooling stride may cause underfitting for the smallest objects, leading to missed detections or lower mAP on specific classes.
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: Oriented RoIs (5D: x, y, w, h, θ) and corresponding RoI features from the backbone feature map (C×H×W) as produced by the oriented RPN
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: lightweight_oriented_roi_head
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: oriented RoIAlign + oriented RCNN head for RoI feature extraction, classification, and regression
modules.output_semantics: Class logits and oriented bounding box offsets (Δx, Δy, Δw, Δh, Δθ) for each RoI, compatible with the baseline post-processing pipeline
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: Reduced inference latency and memory footprint during high-resolution inference on DOTA, with negligible impact on mAP for dense small-object classes (e.g., planes, ships).
modules.proposed_role: lightweight RoI feature extractor and predictor using depthwise separable convolution and global average pooling to reduce compute and memory overhead while preserving detection precision

constraints: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。

research.intended_claim: Replace the standard oriented RoIAlign + oriented RCNN head with a lightweight, channel-compressed RoI feature extractor followed by a single-layer classifier/regressor. The extractor downsamples RoI features via depthwise separable convolution and global average pooling before the final prediction layers, reducing FLOPs and memory bandwidth while preserving spatial precision for small oriented objects.
research.observed_problem: Channel compression and pooling may slightly reduce localization precision for extremely small objects (<10 pixels) if the pooling stride is too aggressive.
research.proposed_mechanism: Depthwise separable convolution reduces spatial and channel-wise computation by factoring standard convolutions into depthwise and pointwise operations, while global average pooling collapses spatial dimensions without learnable parameters, directly cutting FLOPs and memory traffic in the RoI head.
research.scientific_setting: 面向 DOTA 数据集（v1.0/v2.0）的旋转框目标检测任务，聚焦密集小目标（如飞机、船只等）的高分辨率图像（如 1024×1024 及以上）推理优化，不涉及其他数据集或任务变体。
research.success_metric: mAP on DOTA (v1.0 or v2.0 as per evaluation protocol) and inference latency (FPS
research.target_problem: 以论文《Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection》为 Baseline，在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中降低高分辨率推理成本，同时保持旋转框检测精度。

method.problem_method_insight: Oriented R-CNN's oriented RPN already generates high-quality proposals at low cost, but the second-stage RoI refinement and recognition stages still incur significant compute/memory overhead during high-resolution inference, especially in dense small-object scenarios like DOTA. This overhead arises from dense RoI feature extraction and multi-layer perceptron operations over large feature maps.
risks: Oriented R-CNN 在 DOTA 上的基线精度和推理成本数值可能未在公开文献中明确报告，或仅以图表形式呈现，需进一步确认。
现有降低高分辨率推理成本的方法可能主要针对水平框检测（HBB），而非旋转框检测（OBB），适用性存在不确定性。
DOTA 数据集版本（v1.0/v2.0）的选择会影响结果可比性，需在检索时明确版本信息。
高分辨率推理成本与精度的权衡曲线可能依赖于具体硬件平台（如 GPU 型号、内存大小），公开文献中的数值可能不具备直接可比性。
在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中，是否需要优先保证特定类别（如飞机、船只等）的检测精度，还是整体 mAP 为主要优化目标？

metadata.query_text: "Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection"

raw_candidates.snippet: Current state-of-the-art two-stage detectors generate oriented proposals through time-consuming schemes. This diminishes the detectors' speed, thereby becoming the computational bottleneck in advanced oriented object detection systems. This work proposes an effective and simple oriented object detection framework, termed Oriented R-CNN, which is a general two-stage oriented detector with promising accuracy and efficiency. To be specific, in the first stage, we propose an oriented Region Proposal Network (oriented RPN) that directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner. The second stage is oriented R-CNN head for refining oriented Regions of Interest (oriented RoIs) and recognizing them. Without tricks, oriented R-CNN with ResNet50 achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy on two commonly-used datasets for oriented object detection including DOTA (75.87% mAP) and HRSC2016 (96.50% mAP), while having a speed of 15.1 FPS with the image size of 1024$\times$1024 on a single RTX 2080Ti. We hope our work could inspire rethinking the design of oriented detectors and serve as a baseline for oriented object detection. Code is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.
raw_candidates.title: Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection

gap_assessments.summary: The Oriented R-CNN framework and its official implementation repository are explicitly identified and linked in the accepted evidence, confirming the identity of the method and its codebase.

The accepted evidence demonstrates that Oriented R-CNN achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy on DOTA (75.87% mAP) with a speed of 15.1 FPS on a single RTX 2080Ti, indicating efficiency improvements over prior two-stage detectors.

The Oriented RPN in Oriented R-CNN directly generates high-quality oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner, addressing the computational bottleneck in advanced oriented object detection systems.
Oriented R-CNN achieves 75.87% mAP on DOTA with an image size of 1024×1024, demonstrating maintained detection precision in oriented object detection tasks.
The official implementation repository (OBBDetection) provides the code for Oriented R-CNN, enabling reproducibility and further optimization for high-resolution inference scenarios.

limitations: The reported FPS (15.1) and mAP (75.87%) are measured on a single RTX 2080Ti with 1024×1024 images, which may not directly translate to performance on higher-resolution inputs or different hardware.
The evidence does not address the specific impact of high-resolution inference (e.g., >1024×1024) on memory usage or latency, leaving uncertainty for dense small-object scenarios in DOTA.
No explicit evaluation of Oriented R-CNN on modified or optimized inference pipelines (e.g., pruning, quantization) is provided in the accepted evidence.

The Oriented RPN in Oriented R-CNN generates oriented proposals in a nearly cost-free manner, reducing computational overhead.
The official implementation of Oriented R-CNN is available at https://github.com/jbwang1997/OBBDetection.

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy"

items.summary: Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion. Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics. Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series. Our key observation is that due to the rarity of anomalies, it is extremely difficult to build nontrivial associations from abnormal points to the whole series, thereby, the anomalies' associations shall mainly concentrate on their adjacent time points. This adjacent-concentration bias implies an association-based criterion inherently distinguishable between normal and abnormal points, which we highlight through the \emph{Association Discrepancy}. Technically, we propose the \emph{Anomaly Transformer} with a new \emph{Anomaly-Attention} mechanism to compute the association discrepancy. A minimax strategy is devised to amplify the normal-abnormal distinguishability of the association discrepancy. The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space & earth exploration, and water treatment.
items.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: USAD UnSupervised Anomaly Detection multivariate time series architecture loss function constraints

items.summary: We propose an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on a physics-informed diffusion model for multivariate time series data. Over the past years, diffusion model has demonstrated its effectiveness in forecasting, imputation, generation, and anomaly detection in the time series domain. In this paper, we present a new approach for learning the physics-dependent temporal distribution of multivariate time series data using a weighted physics-informed loss during diffusion model training. A weighted physics-informed loss is constructed using a static weight schedule. This approach enables a diffusion model to accurately approximate underlying data distribution, which can influence the unsupervised anomaly detection performance. Our experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that physics-informed training improves the F1 score in anomaly detection; it generates better data diversity and log-likelihood. Our model outperforms baseline approaches, additionally, it surpasses prior physics-informed work and purely data-driven diffusion models on a synthetic dataset and one real-world dataset while remaining competitive on others.
items.title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

items.summary: Detecting anomalies in multivariate time series (MTS) data collected from industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems is essential for a variety of applications, including smart manufacturing. Existing methods typically learn local spatiotemporal representations from nearby time points and neighboring nodes to reconstruct or predict sensor data. However, these local representations are insufficient to model the complex nonlinear topological relationships and dynamic temporal patterns of IIoT systems, which often results in a high-false alarm rate. To address this issue, we propose a new MTS anomaly detection framework called GLAD, which is based on the global–local association discrepancy. The key concept is to detect anomalies based on the difference between the global and local spatiotemporal associations of each data sample, as the association distribution of each data sample provides a more informative description. Specifically, we introduce a Gumbel-Softmax-based graph structure learning strategy to capture the global topological connections from data. Based on the topological graph structure, we utilize a graph attention network (GAT) and transformer to extract both the global and local spatiotemporal associations of each data sample. Finally, we leverage the global–local association discrepancy to effectively detect anomalies from normal data samples. Extensive experiments on five real-world data sets demonstrate the superiority of GLAD over other state-of-the-art methods.
items.title: Global–Local Association Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in IIoT

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

items.summary: Multivariate time series anomaly detection is a longstanding but crucial technology, holding significant potential for system security and stability. Prior studies focus on designing sophisticated architectures, integrating advanced modules (e.g., CNN, LSTM, and Transformer), and identifying anomalies based on point-wise reconstruction errors, as it assumes anomalies cannot be correctly reconstructed. However, reconstruction-based methods are risk of over-generalization, making the assumption untenable. Moreover, the performance of complicated architectures is catastrophically overestimated by the flawed point-adjust protocol. In this paper, we propose a simple but efficient architecture comprising only feed-forward layers. The input time series is hierarchically transformed into multi-scale patches to discover complex temporal information. Triple-views are constructed to capture representation discrepancies among different views as the anomaly criterion, circumventing the overgeneralization issue of reconstruction-based methods. To further amplify the discrepancy, a loss function is designed to encourage the consistency among different views during the training phase. The proposed method is evaluated through quantitative and qualitative experiments, demonstrating its competitive performance on four benchmarks, and providing new baselines to the community without point-adjust protocol.
items.title: Learning Triple-View Representation Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection with Multi-Scale Patching

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

items.summary: Anomaly detection in time series is essential for industrial monitoring and environmental sensing, yet distinguishing anomalies from complex patterns remains challenging. Existing methods like the Anomaly Transformer and DCdetector have progressed, but they face limitations such as sensitivity to short-term contexts and inefficiency in noisy, non-stationary environments. To overcome these issues, we introduce MAAT, an improved architecture that enhances association discrepancy modeling and reconstruction quality. MAAT features Sparse Attention, efficiently capturing long-range dependencies by focusing on relevant time steps, thereby reducing computational redundancy. Additionally, a Mamba-Selective State Space Model is incorporated into the reconstruction module, utilizing a skip connection and Gated Attention to improve anomaly localization and detection performance. Extensive experiments show that MAAT significantly outperforms previous methods, achieving better anomaly distinguishability and generalization across various time series applications, setting a new standard for unsupervised time series anomaly detection in real-world scenarios.
items.title: MAAT: Mamba Adaptive Anomaly Transformer with association discrepancy for time series

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

items.summary: Driven by rapid advancements in big data and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, time series data are now extensively utilized across diverse industrial sectors. The precise identification of anomalies in time series data—especially within intricate and ever-changing environments—has emerged as a key focus in contemporary research. This paper proposes a multivariate anomaly detection framework that synergistically combines variational autoencoders with association discrepancy analysis. By incorporating prior knowledge of associations and sequence association mechanisms, the model can capture long-term dependencies in time series and effectively model the association discrepancy between different time points. Through reconstructing time series data, the model enhances the distinction between normal and anomalous points, learning the association discrepancy during reconstruction to strengthen its ability to identify anomalies. By combining reconstruction errors and association discrepancy, the model achieves more accurate anomaly detection. Extensive experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed methodological framework achieves statistically significant improvements over existing benchmarks, attaining superior F1 scores across diverse public datasets. Notably, it exhibits enhanced capability in modeling temporal dependencies and identifying nuanced anomaly patterns. This work establishes a novel paradigm for time series anomaly detection with profound theoretical implications and practical implementations.
items.title: An Anomaly Detection Method for Multivariate Time Series Data Based on Variational Autoencoders and Association Discrepancy

plan.problem_statement: 判断是否可以将 Anomaly Transformer 中的 Anomaly-Attention 和 association discrepancy 机制接入 USAD 框架，以提升其在 SWaT 工业控制系统多变量时间序列异常检测中的性能。
research_questions: Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 机制在理论上能否与 USAD 的无监督异常检测范式兼容？
现有公开实现中，Anomaly-Attention 是否已被集成至 USAD 或其衍生框架？
SWaT 数据集的多变量时间序列特性是否与 Anomaly-Attention 的假设（如关联偏差）匹配？
接入 Anomaly-Attention 后，USAD 在 SWaT 上的性能提升（如 F1、AUC、延迟）的理论上限与实践可行性如何？
risks: Anomaly-Attention 与 USAD 的损失函数或特征空间设计存在根本性冲突，导致无法直接接入
SWaT 数据集的异常模式与 Anomaly-Attention 的关联偏差假设不匹配，影响性能提升
缺乏公开的 Anomaly-Attention-USAD 集成实现或实验设置，导致复现成本过高
Anomaly-Attention 在 SWaT 上的性能基线未知，无法评估接入后的实际提升
plan.scope: 评估 Anomaly-Attention 与 association discrepancy 在 USAD 架构中的适配性、理论兼容性、实现复杂度及潜在性能提升，并明确在 SWaT 数据集上的实验可行性。

supporting_spans: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy.
Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion.
Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics.
Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series.
We propose an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on a physics-informed diffusion model for multivariate time series data.
Over the past years, diffusion model has demonstrated its effectiveness in forecasting, imputation, generation, and anomaly detection in the time series domain.
Global–Local Association Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in IIoT.
Detecting anomalies in multivariate time series (MTS) data collected from industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems is essential for a variety of applications, including smart manufacturing.
Existing methods typically learn local spatiotemporal representations from nearby time points and neighboring nodes to reconstruct or predict sensor data.
Learning Triple-View Representation Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection with Multi-Scale Patching.
Multivariate time series anomaly detection is a longstanding but crucial technology, holding significant potential for system security and stability.

Prior studies focus on designing sophisticated architectures, integrating advanced modules (e.g., CNN, LSTM, and Transformer), and identifying anomalies based on point-wise reconstruction errors, as it assumes anomalies cannot be correctly reconstructed.
MAAT: Mamba Adaptive Anomaly Transformer with association discrepancy for time series.
Anomaly detection in time series is essential for industrial monitoring and environmental sensing, yet distinguishing anomalies from complex patterns remains challenging.

Existing methods like the Anomaly Transformer and DCdetector have progressed, but they face limitations such as sensitivity to short-term contexts and inefficiency in noisy, non-stationary environments.
An Anomaly Detection Method for Multivariate Time Series Data Based on Variational Autoencoders and Association Discrepancy.
Driven by rapid advancements in big data and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, time series data are now extensively utilized across diverse industrial sectors.
The precise identification of anomalies in time series data—especially within intricate and ever-changing environments—has emerged as a key focus in contemporary research.

research_contract.research_claim: 判断是否可以将 Anomaly Transformer 中的 Anomaly-Attention 和 association discrepancy 机制接入 USAD 框架，以提升其在 SWaT 工业控制系统多变量时间序列异常检测中的性能。

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy"

raw_candidates.snippet: Unsupervised detection of anomaly points in time series is a challenging problem, which requires the model to derive a distinguishable criterion. Previous methods tackle the problem mainly through learning pointwise representation or pairwise association, however, neither is sufficient to reason about the intricate dynamics. Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series. Our key observation is that due to the rarity of anomalies, it is extremely difficult to build nontrivial associations from abnormal points to the whole series, thereby, the anomalies' associations shall mainly concentrate on their adjacent time points. This adjacent-concentration bias implies an association-based criterion inherently distinguishable between normal and abnormal points, which we highlight through the \emph{Association Discrepancy}. Technically, we propose the \emph{Anomaly Transformer} with a new \emph{Anomaly-Attention} mechanism to compute the association discrepancy. A minimax strategy is devised to amplify the normal-abnormal distinguishability of the association discrepancy. The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space & earth exploration, and water treatment.
raw_candidates.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: "Anomaly Transformer Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy" official implementation code repository

Recently, Transformers have shown great power in unified modeling of pointwise representation and pairwise association, and we find that the self-attention weight distribution of each time point can embody rich association with the whole series.
