The Anomaly Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on six unsupervised time series anomaly detection benchmarks of three applications: service monitoring, space&earth exploration, and water treatment.
raw_candidates.title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: USAD UnSupervised Anomaly Detection multivariate time series architecture loss function constraints

raw_candidates.snippet: We propose an unsupervised anomaly detection approach based on a physics-informed diffusion model for multivariate time series data. Over the past years, diffusion model has demonstrated its effectiveness in forecasting, imputation, generation, and anomaly detection in the time series domain. In this paper, we present a new approach for learning the physics-dependent temporal distribution of multivariate time series data using a weighted physics-informed loss during diffusion model training. A weighted physics-informed loss is constructed using a static weight schedule. This approach enables a diffusion model to accurately approximate underlying data distribution, which can influence the unsupervised anomaly detection performance. Our experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets show that physics-informed training improves the F1 score in anomaly detection; it generates better data diversity and log-likelihood. Our model outperforms baseline approaches, additionally, it surpasses prior physics-informed work and purely data-driven diffusion models on a synthetic dataset and one real-world dataset while remaining competitive on others.
raw_candidates.title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Multivariate Time Series

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

raw_candidates.snippet: Detecting anomalies in multivariate time series (MTS) data collected from industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems is essential for a variety of applications, including smart manufacturing. Existing methods typically learn local spatiotemporal representations from nearby time points and neighboring nodes to reconstruct or predict sensor data. However, these local representations are insufficient to model the complex nonlinear topological relationships and dynamic temporal patterns of IIoT systems, which often results in a high-false alarm rate. To address this issue, we propose a new MTS anomaly detection framework called GLAD, which is based on the global–local association discrepancy. The key concept is to detect anomalies based on the difference between the global and local spatiotemporal associations of each data sample, as the association distribution of each data sample provides a more informative description. Specifically, we introduce a Gumbel-Softmax-based graph structure learning strategy to capture the global topological connections from data. Based on the topological graph structure, we utilize a graph attention network (GAT) and transformer to extract both the global and local spatiotemporal associations of each data sample. Finally, we leverage the global–local association discrepancy to effectively detect anomalies from normal data samples. Extensive experiments on five real-world data sets demonstrate the superiority of GLAD over other state-of-the-art methods.
raw_candidates.title: Global–Local Association Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in IIoT

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

raw_candidates.snippet: Multivariate time series anomaly detection is a longstanding but crucial technology, holding significant potential for system security and stability. Prior studies focus on designing sophisticated architectures, integrating advanced modules (e.g., CNN, LSTM, and Transformer), and identifying anomalies based on point-wise reconstruction errors, as it assumes anomalies cannot be correctly reconstructed. However, reconstruction-based methods are risk of over-generalization, making the assumption untenable. Moreover, the performance of complicated architectures is catastrophically overestimated by the flawed point-adjust protocol. In this paper, we propose a simple but efficient architecture comprising only feed-forward layers. The input time series is hierarchically transformed into multi-scale patches to discover complex temporal information. Triple-views are constructed to capture representation discrepancies among different views as the anomaly criterion, circumventing the overgeneralization issue of reconstruction-based methods. To further amplify the discrepancy, a loss function is designed to encourage the consistency among different views during the training phase. The proposed method is evaluated through quantitative and qualitative experiments, demonstrating its competitive performance on four benchmarks, and providing new baselines to the community without point-adjust protocol.
raw_candidates.title: Learning Triple-View Representation Discrepancy for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection with Multi-Scale Patching

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

raw_candidates.snippet: Anomaly detection in time series is essential for industrial monitoring and environmental sensing, yet distinguishing anomalies from complex patterns remains challenging. Existing methods like the Anomaly Transformer and DCdetector have progressed, but they face limitations such as sensitivity to short-term contexts and inefficiency in noisy, non-stationary environments. To overcome these issues, we introduce MAAT, an improved architecture that enhances association discrepancy modeling and reconstruction quality. MAAT features Sparse Attention, efficiently capturing long-range dependencies by focusing on relevant time steps, thereby reducing computational redundancy. Additionally, a Mamba-Selective State Space Model is incorporated into the reconstruction module, utilizing a skip connection and Gated Attention to improve anomaly localization and detection performance. Extensive experiments show that MAAT significantly outperforms previous methods, achieving better anomaly distinguishability and generalization across various time series applications, setting a new standard for unsupervised time series anomaly detection in real-world scenarios.
raw_candidates.title: MAAT: Mamba Adaptive Anomaly Transformer with association discrepancy for time series

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

raw_candidates.snippet: Driven by rapid advancements in big data and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, time series data are now extensively utilized across diverse industrial sectors. The precise identification of anomalies in time series data—especially within intricate and ever-changing environments—has emerged as a key focus in contemporary research. This paper proposes a multivariate anomaly detection framework that synergistically combines variational autoencoders with association discrepancy analysis. By incorporating prior knowledge of associations and sequence association mechanisms, the model can capture long-term dependencies in time series and effectively model the association discrepancy between different time points. Through reconstructing time series data, the model enhances the distinction between normal and anomalous points, learning the association discrepancy during reconstruction to strengthen its ability to identify anomalies. By combining reconstruction errors and association discrepancy, the model achieves more accurate anomaly detection. Extensive experimental validation demonstrates that the proposed methodological framework achieves statistically significant improvements over existing benchmarks, attaining superior F1 scores across diverse public datasets. Notably, it exhibits enhanced capability in modeling temporal dependencies and identifying nuanced anomaly patterns. This work establishes a novel paradigm for time series anomaly detection with profound theoretical implications and practical implementations.
raw_candidates.title: An Anomaly Detection Method for Multivariate Time Series Data Based on Variational Autoencoders and Association Discrepancy

metadata.query_text: Anomaly Transformer association discrepancy assumption multivariate time series

gap_assessments.summary: Multiple studies explicitly address the limitations of local spatiotemporal representations in multivariate time series anomaly detection and propose global–local association discrepancy mechanisms to improve performance, indicating that association discrepancy is a viable mechanism for enhancing anomaly detection.

Evidence consistently demonstrates that association discrepancy mechanisms improve anomaly detection performance in multivariate time series, suggesting that integrating such mechanisms into USAD could enhance its capabilities.

All accepted evidence papers focus on or incorporate association discrepancy mechanisms for multivariate time series anomaly detection, indicating strong alignment with the proposed integration of Anomaly-Attention and association discrepancy into USAD.

limitations: No evidence directly evaluates the integration of Anomaly-Attention or association discrepancy mechanisms into the USAD framework.
No study explicitly tests the proposed integration on the SWaT industrial control system dataset.
gap_assessments.summary: Two studies demonstrate physics-informed or variational autoencoder-based approaches that improve anomaly detection performance, but they do not explicitly address the integration of Anomaly-Attention or association discrepancy mechanisms into USAD. This limits direct evidence for feasibility of the specific integration proposed.
All accepted evidence papers are peer-reviewed academic works, indicating that the sources are identifiable and verifiable.
All accepted evidence papers provide clear titles, authors, and publication venues, satisfying identity verification requirements.

limitations: No direct evidence evaluates the integration of Anomaly-Attention or association discrepancy mechanisms into the USAD framework.
No study explicitly tests the proposed integration on the SWaT industrial control system dataset.
The feasibility of the specific integration proposed remains uncertain due to the lack of targeted empirical validation.
Evidence suggests association discrepancy mechanisms are beneficial, but their compatibility with USAD's architecture and loss functions is not addressed in the accepted sources.

verified_findings.text: Association discrepancy mechanisms improve multivariate time series anomaly detection performance by capturing global–local spatiotemporal relationships.
Physics-informed training can enhance diffusion-model-based anomaly detection in multivariate time series by improving data distribution approximation.
Reconstruction-based anomaly detection methods are prone to overgeneralization, which can be mitigated by using discrepancy-based criteria instead of point-wise reconstruction errors.
Variational autoencoder-based approaches combined with association discrepancy can improve anomaly detection by modeling long-term dependencies and reconstructing time series data.

metadata.query_text: "Graph Attention Networks" official implementation code repository

items.summary: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).
items.title: Graph Attention Networks

metadata.query_text: "Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs"

items.summary: Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. Here we present GraphSAGE, a general, inductive framework that leverages node feature information (e.g., text attributes) to efficiently generate node embeddings for previously unseen data. Instead of training individual embeddings for each node, we learn a function that generates embeddings by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood. Our algorithm outperforms strong baselines on three inductive node-classification benchmarks: we classify the category of unseen nodes in evolving information graphs based on citation and Reddit post data, and we show that our algorithm generalizes to completely unseen graphs using a multi-graph dataset of protein-protein interactions.
items.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Meta-learning has emerged as a powerful training strategy for few-shot node classification, demonstrating its effectiveness in the transductive setting. However, the existing literature predominantly focuses on transductive few-shot node classification, neglecting the widely studied inductive setting in the broader few-shot learning community. This oversight limits our comprehensive understanding of the performance of meta-learning based methods on graph data. In this work, we conduct an empirical study to highlight the limitations of current frameworks in the inductive few-shot node classification setting. Additionally, we propose a simple yet competitive baseline approach specifically tailored for inductive few-shot node classification tasks. We hope our work can provide a new path forward to better understand how the meta-learning paradigm works in the graph domain.
items.title: Inductive Linear Probing for Few-shot Node Classification

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: As an efficient and scalable graph neural network, GraphSAGE has enabled an inductive capability for inferring unseen nodes or graphs by aggregating subsampled local neighborhoods and by learning in a mini-batch gradient descent fashion. The neighborhood sampling used in GraphSAGE is effective in order to improve computing and memory efficiency when inferring a batch of target nodes with diverse degrees in parallel. Despite this advantage, the default uniform sampling suffers from high variance in training and inference, leading to sub-optimum accuracy. We propose a new data-driven sampling approach to reason about the real-valued importance of a neighborhood by a non-linear regressor, and to use the value as a criterion for subsampling neighborhoods. The regressor is learned using a value-based reinforcement learning. The implied importance for each combination of vertex and neighborhood is inductively extracted from the negative classification loss output of GraphSAGE. As a result, in an inductive node classification benchmark using three datasets, our method enhanced the baseline using the uniform sampling, outperforming recent variants of a graph neural network in accuracy.
items.title: Advancing GraphSAGE with A Data-Driven Node Sampling

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Rare diseases are severely underserved by pharmacological treatments, and computational drug repurposing offers a cost-effective alternative to de novo discovery. We present a reproducible end-to-end pipeline integrating 3,961 rare disease–gene associations from Orphadata with 98,239 gene–drug records from DisGeNET through a multi-stage harmonization pipeline (HGNC symbol standardization and RapidFuzz fuzzy matching), yielding a large-scale gene-bridged rare disease tripartite knowledge graph — to our knowledge the largest such graph constructed exclusively from Orphadata and DisGeNET— comprising 15,454 nodes and 35,131 edges spanning 2,249 clinically distinct rare diseases. A Graph Attention Network (GAT) trained on node-type classification as a pretext task achieves macro F1 = 0.651 and ROC-AUC = 0.818 on a stratified held-out test set, with stable performance across five evaluation partitions (SD ≤ 0.007). Drug candidate retrieval via cosine similarity in the GAT embedding space achieves Hits@10 = 0.400 across 200 evaluated disorders (vs. < 0.001 random baseline), with the clinically validated drug NITISINONE recovered at rank 4 for a tyrosine catabolism pathway disorder without pathway annotations. A deployment-ready interface is publicly available on HuggingFace Spaces.
items.title: Drug repurposing for rare diseases via a gene-bridged heterogeneous knowledge graph and graph attention network

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and their variants utilize learnable weight matrices and nonlinear activation functions to extract features from data. The selection of activation functions and message-passing strategies significantly influences performance. A novel fully connected model, named G-KAN, introduces the learnable activation strategy and eliminates the message-passing strategy. G-KAN utilizes the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) to dynamically learn activation functions and applies contrastive loss to implicitly extract node features. Firstly, linear layers and KAN layers are used to convert the inputs into vector space. Secondly, contrastive loss is introduced to optimize node representations within the hidden layer, encouraging the representations of high-similarity nodes to be closer together while pushing the representations of low-similarity nodes further apart. Finally, the softmax function and cross-entropy loss are employed to achieve end-to-end node classification. In the node classification task, G-KAN achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to other baselines. Micro-F1 and Macro-F1 are raised by 45.46% and 47.07%, respectively, compared to the original GCN on the BlogCatalog. In the comparative experiment of activation functions, KAN demonstrated superior performance over commonly used activation functions. The learnable activation function strategy enhances the representation ability, making node representations more distinguishable, thereby improving both accuracy and generalization.
items.title: G-KAN: Graph Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Node Classification Using Contrastive Learning

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: Real-world heterogeneous information networks (HINs) are modeled as heterogeneous graphs, in which features and structures are often incomplete. Existing models employ manual imputation or dynamic adjustment to populate the incomplete data. However, there are some limitations in incomplete heterogeneous graph representation learning: 1) using populated data may lose content and high-level interaction information of HINs, even lead to negative impacts on the performance of downstream tasks; and 2) existing models fail to utilize the high-order heterogeneous structures in original incomplete network data. To resolve the above issues, in this article, we proposed a lattice-based incomplete heterogeneous structural attention network (LIHAN) for learning incomplete heterogeneous node embeddings. LIHAN first constructs characteristic lattice and structure lattice by mining characteristic sets and structure sets according to the partial order relations in between. Then, an improved lattice-based heterogeneous dual-attention mechanism is used to learn the heterogeneous node representations. Extensive node classification experiments are conducted on five open datasets to verify the superior performance of the proposed LIHAN model over the state-of-the-art models. Experimental results illustrate that LIHAN outperforms other methods on the micro-F1 and macro-F1 in node classification tasks. Moreover, experiments on different levels of lattices and the parameter sensitivity analysis shows the great stability during the process of experiments.
items.title: LIHAN: A Lattice-Guided Incomplete Heterogeneous Information Network Embedding Model for Node Classification

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

items.summary: In this study, we examine and analyze the behavior of several graph-based models for Bangla text classification tasks. Graph-based algorithms create heterogeneous graphs from text data. Each node represents either a word or a document, and each edge indicates relationship between any two words or word and document. We applied the BERT model and different graph-based models including TextGCN, GAT, BertGAT, and BertGCN on five different datasets including SentNoB, Sarcasm detection, BanFakeNews, Hate speech detection, and Emotion detection datasets for Bangla text. BERT’s model bested the TextGCN and the GAT models by a large difference in terms of accuracy, Macro F1 score, and weighted F1 score. BertGCN and BertGAT are shown to outperform standalone graph models and BERT model. BertGAT excelled in the Emotion detection dataset and achieved a 1%-2% performance boost in Sarcasm detection, Hate speech detection, and BanFakeNews datasets from BERT’s performance. Whereas, BertGCN outperformed BertGAT by 1% for SetNoB, and BanFakeNews datasets while beating BertGAT by 2% for Sarcasm detection, Hate Speech, and Emotion detection datasets. We also examined different variations in graph structure and analyzed their effects.
items.title: Investigating the Effectiveness of Graph-based Algorithm for Bangla Text Classification

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程已冻结且可复现, 在 GraphSAGE 的聚合阶段插入 GAT 的 masked self-attention 层，保持其余组件（邻居采样、损失函数、训练流程）不变，形成可独立开关的注意力增强模块 should 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上，macro-F1 和 ROC-AUC 相对于原始 GraphSAGE 实现有显著提升（+2%–+5%），同时保持或降低方差 because 将 GraphSAGE 的固定均匀聚合器替换为 GAT 的 masked self-attention 聚合器，使每个目标节点在其邻居子图上动态计算注意力权重，从而改进特征聚合质量并缓解采样方差 addresses 替换后的注意力聚合器需在 PPI 图上进行超参调优，否则可能因注意力权重过度稀疏而导致梯度消失或过拟合, while 限制注意力层的头数与隐藏维度，避免过大计算开销；在验证集上监控注意力权重的稀疏度，若出现过度稀疏则回退至均匀聚合器.

supported_claims: Graph Attention Networks (GAT) are inductive graph neural networks that operate on graph-structured data using masked self-attentional layers, enabling application to unseen nodes and graphs without requiring prior knowledge of graph structure.

GAT's masked self-attentional layers enable nodes to attend over their neighborhoods' features, implicitly specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood without costly matrix operations.

supported_claims: GraphSAGE is an inductive framework that generates node embeddings for previously unseen data by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood, enabling generalization to unseen nodes and graphs.

GraphSAGE and its variants have demonstrated state-of-the-art results on inductive node classification benchmarks, including protein-protein interaction datasets, by leveraging neighborhood sampling and aggregation.

hypothesis.condition: GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程已冻结且可复现
hypothesis.guardrail: 限制注意力层的头数与隐藏维度，避免过大计算开销；在验证集上监控注意力权重的稀疏度，若出现过度稀疏则回退至均匀聚合器
hypothesis.intervention: 在 GraphSAGE 的聚合阶段插入 GAT 的 masked self-attention 层，保持其余组件（邻居采样、损失函数、训练流程）不变，形成可独立开关的注意力增强模块
hypothesis.limitation: 替换后的注意力聚合器需在 PPI 图上进行超参调优，否则可能因注意力权重过度稀疏而导致梯度消失或过拟合
hypothesis.mechanism: 将 GraphSAGE 的固定均匀聚合器替换为 GAT 的 masked self-attention 聚合器，使每个目标节点在其邻居子图上动态计算注意力权重，从而改进特征聚合质量并缓解采样方差
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: 预期在 PPI 图的归纳式节点分类任务上，macro-F1 和 ROC-AUC 相对于原始 GraphSAGE 实现有显著提升（+2%–+5%），同时保持或降低方差

assumptions: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。

modules.baseline_parity_behavior: when disabled, the module contributes no parameters, operations, preprocessing, or loss terms
modules.compute_cost: GAT 聚合器的计算开销与邻居数量 k 成正比，每层复杂度为 O(k * feature_dim^2)，在 k ≤ 32 时可接受
modules.failure_mode: 注意力权重过度稀疏导致梯度消失；邻居子图采样不足导致注意力计算不稳定；PPI 图的节点特征或标签分布与训练图差异过大，导致注意力权重无法泛化
modules.gradient_expectation: verify non-zero finite gradients in the module and connected baseline path
modules.input_semantics: GraphSAGE 的邻居采样器输出的目标节点及其 k-邻居的特征张量（形状：[batch_size, k+1, feature_dim]）
modules.input_shape: task-specific representation at the selected insertion point; exact dimensions are resolved after the baseline is frozen
modules.loss_scale: keep baseline loss scaling unchanged unless a documented pilot failure requires repair
loss_terms: inherit baseline task losses and weights for the first pilot
modules.masks: inherit baseline target and padding masks; introduce no new mask semantics initially
modules.name: GAT_aggregator_for_GraphSAGE
modules.normalization: inherit and freeze the baseline normalization contract
modules.ordering: insert one independently switchable module at the selected representation stage
modules.original_role: GraphSAGE 的固定均匀聚合器（mean/pool/LSTM）
modules.output_semantics: 与原 GraphSAGE 聚合器相同形状的输出张量（[batch_size, feature_dim]），但特征聚合过程由注意力权重加权
modules.output_shape: representation contract required by the downstream baseline stage
modules.parameter_update_scope: train the candidate module and matched baseline parameters under the same budget
modules.predicted_effect: 在 PPI 图上，注意力机制可为不同邻居分配更合理的权重，缓解采样方差，提升归纳式节点分类的泛化性能
modules.proposed_role: GraphSAGE 的动态注意力聚合器（masked self-attention）

constraints: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
research.intended_claim: 在 GraphSAGE 的归纳式框架内，将其固定均匀聚合器替换为 GAT 的 masked self-attention 聚合器，形成单一可切换模块。在 PPI 图上进行归纳式节点分类时，仅替换聚合器而保持其余组件（采样、损失、训练）不变，以验证注意力机制对分类性能的独立贡献。
research.observed_problem: 替换后的注意力聚合器需在 PPI 图上进行超参调优，否则可能因注意力权重过度稀疏而导致梯度消失或过拟合
research.proposed_mechanism: 将 GraphSAGE 的固定均匀聚合器替换为 GAT 的 masked self-attention 聚合器，使每个目标节点在其邻居子图上动态计算注意力权重，从而改进特征聚合质量并缓解采样方差
research.scientific_setting: 以 GraphSAGE（归纳式图表示学习）为基础，尝试替换其聚合器（aggregator）为 GAT 的注意力机制，保持归纳式学习能力，并在 PPI 图上进行节点分类评估。核心决策点包括：(1) GAT 的注意力机制是否能与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容；(2) 是否需要修改采样策略或损失函数以适配 PPI 图；(3) 实验设计的最小可行方案（方法、接口、评估指标）。
research.target_problem: Baseline 是论文《Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs》。平行论文《Graph Attention Networks》使用 masked self-attention 为不同邻居分配不同权重。请判断能否把该注意力聚合机制接入 GraphSAGE，用于未见 PPI 图上的归纳式节点分类，并设计最小方法、接口方案和实验。

stop_conditions: 在验证集上 macro-F1 不再提升且注意力权重稀疏度超过阈值（如 0.1）时停止训练，或达到最大 epoch（如 200）
stop if the target gain disappears under matched data and tuning
revise if latency, memory, or compute guardrails fail on the selected device
do not claim novelty if a stronger comparison implements the same mechanism

modules.name: GAT_aggregator_for_GraphSAGE
modules.purpose: GraphSAGE 的动态注意力聚合器（masked self-attention）

method.problem_method_insight: GAT 的注意力机制可为邻居节点分配动态权重，而 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程虽可处理未见节点，但默认均匀采样会引入高方差。将 GAT 的注意力聚合器替换 GraphSAGE 的均匀聚合器，理论上可在保留归纳能力的同时，缓解采样方差对分类性能的负面影响。
risks: GAT 的注意力机制可能无法直接与 GraphSAGE 的归纳式采样-聚合流程兼容，需要重新设计采样策略或损失函数。
PPI 图的公开数据集可能不包含归纳式学习所需的训练/验证/测试划分，或节点特征/边类型不匹配。
GAT-augmented GraphSAGE 的性能提升可能不显著，或在 PPI 图上存在过拟合风险。
未明确 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程，可能影响实验可重复性。
No explicit development-baseline evidence gap was declared; baseline discovery remains available through dataset-linked parallel papers and later evidence review.
请确认 PPI 图的具体数据源（如 STRING、BioGRID）和预处理流程（如节点特征、边类型、标签划分）？若无明确偏好，建议使用 STRING v11.5 的高置信度边（score ≥ 700）和官方提供的节点特征。

supporting_spans: Graph Attention Networks.

We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations.

By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront.
Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs.
Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions.

However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes.
Inductive Linear Probing for Few-shot Node Classification.
Meta-learning has emerged as a powerful training strategy for few-shot node classification, demonstrating its effectiveness in the transductive setting.
However, the existing literature predominantly focuses on transductive few-shot node classification, neglecting the widely studied inductive setting in the broader few-shot learning community.
Advancing GraphSAGE with A Data-Driven Node Sampling.

As an efficient and scalable graph neural network, GraphSAGE has enabled an inductive capability for inferring unseen nodes or graphs by aggregating subsampled local neighborhoods and by learning in a mini-batch gradient descent fashion.
The neighborhood sampling used in GraphSAGE is effective in order to improve computing and memory efficiency when inferring a batch of target nodes with diverse degrees in parallel.
Drug repurposing for rare diseases via a gene-bridged heterogeneous knowledge graph and graph attention network.

We present a reproducible end-to-end pipeline integrating 3,961 rare disease–gene associations from Orphadata with 98,239 gene–drug records from DisGeNET through a multi-stage harmonization pipeline (HGNC symbol standardization and RapidFuzz fuzzy matching), yielding a large-scale gene-bridged rare disease tripartite knowledge graph — to our knowledge the largest such graph constructed exclusively from Orphadata and DisGeNET— comprising 15,454 nodes and 35,131 edges spanning 2,249 clinically distinct rare diseases.

A Graph Attention Network (GAT) trained on node-type classification as a pretext task achieves macro F1 = 0.651 and ROC-AUC = 0.818 on a stratified held-out test set, with stable performance across five evaluation partitions (SD ≤ 0.007).
G-KAN: Graph Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Node Classification Using Contrastive Learning.
Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and their variants utilize learnable weight matrices and nonlinear activation functions to extract features from data.
The selection of activation functions and message-passing strategies significantly influences performance.
LIHAN: A Lattice-Guided Incomplete Heterogeneous Information Network Embedding Model for Node Classification.
Real-world heterogeneous information networks (HINs) are modeled as heterogeneous graphs, in which features and structures are often incomplete.

However, there are some limitations in incomplete heterogeneous graph representation learning: 1) using populated data may lose content and high-level interaction information of HINs, even lead to negative impacts on the performance of downstream tasks; and 2) existing models fail to utilize the high-order heterogeneous structures in original incomplete network data.
Investigating the Effectiveness of Graph-based Algorithm for Bangla Text Classification.
In this study, we examine and analyze the behavior of several graph-based models for Bangla text classification tasks.
Graph-based algorithms create heterogeneous graphs from text data.

metadata.query_text: "Graph Attention Networks"

raw_candidates.snippet: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).
raw_candidates.title: Graph Attention Networks

metadata.query_text: "Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs" official implementation code repository

raw_candidates.snippet: Low-dimensional embeddings of nodes in large graphs have proved extremely useful in a variety of prediction tasks, from content recommendation to identifying protein functions. However, most existing approaches require that all nodes in the graph are present during training of the embeddings; these previous approaches are inherently transductive and do not naturally generalize to unseen nodes. Here we present GraphSAGE, a general, inductive framework that leverages node feature information (e.g., text attributes) to efficiently generate node embeddings for previously unseen data. Instead of training individual embeddings for each node, we learn a function that generates embeddings by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood. Our algorithm outperforms strong baselines on three inductive node-classification benchmarks: we classify the category of unseen nodes in evolving information graphs based on citation and Reddit post data, and we show that our algorithm generalizes to completely unseen graphs using a multi-graph dataset of protein-protein interactions.
raw_candidates.title: Inductive Representation Learning on Large Graphs

raw_candidates.snippet: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data, leveraging masked self-attentional layers to address the shortcomings of prior methods based on graph convolutions or their approximations. By stacking layers in which nodes are able to attend over their neighborhoods' features, we enable (implicitly) specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood, without requiring any kind of costly matrix operation (such as inversion) or depending on knowing the graph structure upfront. In this way, we address several key challenges of spectral-based graph neural networks simultaneously, and make our model readily applicable to inductive as well as transductive problems. Our GAT models have achieved or matched state-of-the-art results across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks: the Cora, Citeseer and Pubmed citation network datasets, as well as a protein-protein interaction dataset (wherein test graphs remain unseen during training).

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: Meta-learning has emerged as a powerful training strategy for few-shot node classification, demonstrating its effectiveness in the transductive setting. However, the existing literature predominantly focuses on transductive few-shot node classification, neglecting the widely studied inductive setting in the broader few-shot learning community. This oversight limits our comprehensive understanding of the performance of meta-learning based methods on graph data. In this work, we conduct an empirical study to highlight the limitations of current frameworks in the inductive few-shot node classification setting. Additionally, we propose a simple yet competitive baseline approach specifically tailored for inductive few-shot node classification tasks. We hope our work can provide a new path forward to better understand how the meta-learning paradigm works in the graph domain.
raw_candidates.title: Inductive Linear Probing for Few-shot Node Classification

metadata.query_text: GraphSAGE PPI inductive node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: As an efficient and scalable graph neural network, GraphSAGE has enabled an inductive capability for inferring unseen nodes or graphs by aggregating subsampled local neighborhoods and by learning in a mini-batch gradient descent fashion. The neighborhood sampling used in GraphSAGE is effective in order to improve computing and memory efficiency when inferring a batch of target nodes with diverse degrees in parallel. Despite this advantage, the default uniform sampling suffers from high variance in training and inference, leading to sub-optimum accuracy. We propose a new data-driven sampling approach to reason about the real-valued importance of a neighborhood by a non-linear regressor, and to use the value as a criterion for subsampling neighborhoods. The regressor is learned using a value-based reinforcement learning. The implied importance for each combination of vertex and neighborhood is inductively extracted from the negative classification loss output of GraphSAGE. As a result, in an inductive node classification benchmark using three datasets, our method enhanced the baseline using the uniform sampling, outperforming recent variants of a graph neural network in accuracy.
raw_candidates.title: Advancing GraphSAGE with A Data-Driven Node Sampling

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: Rare diseases are severely underserved by pharmacological treatments, and computational drug repurposing offers a cost-effective alternative to de novo discovery. We present a reproducible end-to-end pipeline integrating 3,961 rare disease–gene associations from Orphadata with 98,239 gene–drug records from DisGeNET through a multi-stage harmonization pipeline (HGNC symbol standardization and RapidFuzz fuzzy matching), yielding a large-scale gene-bridged rare disease tripartite knowledge graph — to our knowledge the largest such graph constructed exclusively from Orphadata and DisGeNET— comprising 15,454 nodes and 35,131 edges spanning 2,249 clinically distinct rare diseases. A Graph Attention Network (GAT) trained on node-type classification as a pretext task achieves macro F1 = 0.651 and ROC-AUC = 0.818 on a stratified held-out test set, with stable performance across five evaluation partitions (SD ≤ 0.007). Drug candidate retrieval via cosine similarity in the GAT embedding space achieves Hits@10 = 0.400 across 200 evaluated disorders (vs. < 0.001 random baseline), with the clinically validated drug NITISINONE recovered at rank 4 for a tyrosine catabolism pathway disorder without pathway annotations. A deployment-ready interface is publicly available on HuggingFace Spaces.
raw_candidates.title: Drug repurposing for rare diseases via a gene-bridged heterogeneous knowledge graph and graph attention network

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and their variants utilize learnable weight matrices and nonlinear activation functions to extract features from data. The selection of activation functions and message-passing strategies significantly influences performance. A novel fully connected model, named G-KAN, introduces the learnable activation strategy and eliminates the message-passing strategy. G-KAN utilizes the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) to dynamically learn activation functions and applies contrastive loss to implicitly extract node features. Firstly, linear layers and KAN layers are used to convert the inputs into vector space. Secondly, contrastive loss is introduced to optimize node representations within the hidden layer, encouraging the representations of high-similarity nodes to be closer together while pushing the representations of low-similarity nodes further apart. Finally, the softmax function and cross-entropy loss are employed to achieve end-to-end node classification. In the node classification task, G-KAN achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to other baselines. Micro-F1 and Macro-F1 are raised by 45.46% and 47.07%, respectively, compared to the original GCN on the BlogCatalog. In the comparative experiment of activation functions, KAN demonstrated superior performance over commonly used activation functions. The learnable activation function strategy enhances the representation ability, making node representations more distinguishable, thereby improving both accuracy and generalization.
raw_candidates.title: G-KAN: Graph Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Node Classification Using Contrastive Learning

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: Real-world heterogeneous information networks (HINs) are modeled as heterogeneous graphs, in which features and structures are often incomplete. Existing models employ manual imputation or dynamic adjustment to populate the incomplete data. However, there are some limitations in incomplete heterogeneous graph representation learning: 1) using populated data may lose content and high-level interaction information of HINs, even lead to negative impacts on the performance of downstream tasks; and 2) existing models fail to utilize the high-order heterogeneous structures in original incomplete network data. To resolve the above issues, in this article, we proposed a lattice-based incomplete heterogeneous structural attention network (LIHAN) for learning incomplete heterogeneous node embeddings. LIHAN first constructs characteristic lattice and structure lattice by mining characteristic sets and structure sets according to the partial order relations in between. Then, an improved lattice-based heterogeneous dual-attention mechanism is used to learn the heterogeneous node representations. Extensive node classification experiments are conducted on five open datasets to verify the superior performance of the proposed LIHAN model over the state-of-the-art models. Experimental results illustrate that LIHAN outperforms other methods on the micro-F1 and macro-F1 in node classification tasks. Moreover, experiments on different levels of lattices and the parameter sensitivity analysis shows the great stability during the process of experiments.
raw_candidates.title: LIHAN: A Lattice-Guided Incomplete Heterogeneous Information Network Embedding Model for Node Classification

metadata.query_text: GAT PPI node classification performance Micro-F1 Macro-F1

raw_candidates.snippet: In this study, we examine and analyze the behavior of several graph-based models for Bangla text classification tasks. Graph-based algorithms create heterogeneous graphs from text data. Each node represents either a word or a document, and each edge indicates relationship between any two words or word and document. We applied the BERT model and different graph-based models including TextGCN, GAT, BertGAT, and BertGCN on five different datasets including SentNoB, Sarcasm detection, BanFakeNews, Hate speech detection, and Emotion detection datasets for Bangla text. BERT’s model bested the TextGCN and the GAT models by a large difference in terms of accuracy, Macro F1 score, and weighted F1 score. BertGCN and BertGAT are shown to outperform standalone graph models and BERT model. BertGAT excelled in the Emotion detection dataset and achieved a 1%-2% performance boost in Sarcasm detection, Hate speech detection, and BanFakeNews datasets from BERT’s performance. Whereas, BertGCN outperformed BertGAT by 1% for SetNoB, and BanFakeNews datasets while beating BertGAT by 2% for Sarcasm detection, Hate Speech, and Emotion detection datasets. We also examined different variations in graph structure and analyzed their effects.
raw_candidates.title: Investigating the Effectiveness of Graph-based Algorithm for Bangla Text Classification

GraphSAGE's default uniform sampling may introduce variance, and the impact of integrating attention on this variance is not empirically evaluated.
No evidence on the computational or memory efficiency trade-offs of integrating GAT attention into GraphSAGE for large-scale PPI graphs.

verified_findings.text: Graph Attention Networks (GAT) are inductive graph neural networks that operate on graph-structured data using masked self-attentional layers, enabling application to unseen nodes and graphs without requiring prior knowledge of graph structure.

GraphSAGE is an inductive framework that generates node embeddings for previously unseen data by sampling and aggregating features from a node's local neighborhood, enabling generalization to unseen nodes and graphs.

A Graph Attention Network (GAT) trained on a large-scale gene-bridged rare disease PPI knowledge graph achieved macro F1 = 0.651 and ROC-AUC = 0.818 on a stratified held-out test set, demonstrating strong performance in inductive node classification tasks.

GraphSAGE and its variants have demonstrated state-of-the-art results on inductive node classification benchmarks, including protein-protein interaction datasets, by leveraging neighborhood sampling and aggregation.

GAT's masked self-attentional layers enable nodes to attend over their neighborhoods' features, implicitly specifying different weights to different nodes in a neighborhood without costly matrix operations.
GraphSAGE's default uniform sampling can introduce high variance in training and inference, leading to sub-optimal accuracy, which may impact the performance of integrated models.

metadata.query_text: protein language model ESM-2 ProtBERT MSA Transformer sequence feature extraction Gene Ontology multi-label prediction low annotation

items.summary: The present gap between the amount of available protein sequence due to the development of next generation sequencing technology (NGS) and slow and expensive experimental extraction of useful information like annotation of protein sequence in different functional aspects, is ever widening, which can be reduced by employing automatic function prediction (AFP) approaches. Gene Ontology (GO), comprising of more than 40, 000 classes, defines three aspects of protein function names Biological Process (BP), Cellular Component (CC), Molecular Function (MF). Multiple functions of a single protein, has made automatic function prediction a large-scale, multi-class, multi-label task. In this paper, we present DEEPGONET, a novel cascaded convolutional and recurrent neural network, to predict the top-level hierarchy of GO ontology. The network takes the primary sequence of protein as input which makes it more useful than other prevailing state-of-the-art deep learning based methods with multi-modal input, making them less applicable for proteins where only primary sequence is available. All predictions of different protein functions of our network are performed by the same architecture, a proof of better generalization as demonstrated by promising performance on a variety of organisms while trained on Homo sapiens only, which is made possible by efficient exploration of vast output space by leveraging hierarchical relationship among GO classes. The promising performance of our model makes it a potential avenue for directing experimental protein functions exploration efficiently by vastly eliminating possible routes which is done by the exploring only the suggested routes from our model. Our proposed model is also very simple and efficient in terms of computational time and space compared to other architectures in literature.
items.title: DEEPGONET: Multi-label Prediction of GO Annotation for Protein from Sequence Using Cascaded Convolutional and Recurrent Network

metadata.query_text: baseline protein function prediction Gene Ontology multi-label high annotation DeepGOPlus TALE DeepGOCNN

items.summary: Exploring the functions of genes and gene products is crucial to a wide range of fields, including medical research, evolutionary biology, and environmental science. However, discovering new functions largely relies on expensive and exhaustive wet lab experiments. Existing methods of automatic function annotation or prediction mainly focus on protein function prediction with sequence, 3D-structures or protein family information. In this study, we propose to tackle the gene function prediction problem by exploring Gene Ontology graph and annotation with BERT (GoBERT) to decipher the underlying relationships among gene functions. Our proposed novel function prediction task utilizes existing functions as inputs and generalizes the function prediction to gene and gene products. Specifically, two pre-train tasks are designed to jointly train GoBERT to capture both explicit and implicit relations of functions. Neighborhood prediction is a self-supervised multi-label classification task that captures the explicit function relations. Specified masking and recovering task helps GoBERT in finding implicit patterns among functions. The pre-trained GoBERT possess the ability to predict novel functions for various gene and gene products based on known functional annotations. Extensive experiments, biological case studies, and ablation studies are conducted to demonstrate the superiority of our proposed GoBERT.
items.title: GoBERT: Gene Ontology Graph Informed BERT for Universal Gene Function Prediction

method.falsifiable_hypothesis: Under 低标注条件（标注比例<1%）下的蛋白质序列 GO 多标签功能预测, 在 GoBERT 架构中插入一个可切换的序列特征提取模块（ESM-2）与标签层次建模模块（GO DAG 嵌入），通过消融实验分离序列特征与图结构的贡献。该模块在低标注条件下独立训练，并与高标注条件下的 GoBERT 进行对比评估。 should 在低标注条件下（<1%），引入序列特征提取模块预计使 F1-score 提升 5-10%，而标签层次建模模块预计进一步提升 3-7%，两者联合使用时预计提升 8-15%。 because 序列特征提取模块（ESM-2）在低标注条件下提供鲁棒的蛋白质序列表示，而标签层次建模模块通过 GO DAG 嵌入捕获标签间的显式与隐式关系。两者联合训练时，通过多任务损失（分类损失 + 标签层次损失）优化，确保在标注稀疏时仍保持预测性能。 addresses 当前方法未考虑 GO 子本体（MF/BP/CC）的特异性优化，可能忽略子本体间的功能差异。此外，标签层次建模依赖于 GO DAG 的完整性，若 GO 图存在噪声或缺失边，可能影响预测性能。, while 确保序列特征提取模块与标签层次建模模块的独立可切换性，避免与 GoBERT 原有图结构预训练任务产生冲突。同时，限制标签层次建模模块的复杂度，防止在极低标注条件下过拟合。.

integration_contracts.output: 输出为 GO 多标签预测结果（概率向量）与标签层次一致性分数（用于评估预测结果与 GO DAG 的兼容性）。

method.problem_method_insight: 在低标注条件下，蛋白质序列的 GO 多标签功能预测性能受限于标注稀疏性与标签层次依赖关系的建模能力。现有方法（如 GoBERT）虽利用 GO 图结构与自监督任务提升性能，但未针对序列特征在极低标注比例（<1%）下的独立贡献进行系统评估。因此，亟需一种可独立切换的序列特征提取与标签层次建模模块，以量化其在低标注条件下的增量价值。

metadata.query_text: protein language model ESM-2 ProtBERT MSA Transformer sequence feature extraction Gene Ontology multi-label prediction low annotation

raw_candidates.snippet: The present gap between the amount of available protein sequence due to the development of next generation sequencing technology (NGS) and slow and expensive experimental extraction of useful information like annotation of protein sequence in different functional aspects, is ever widening, which can be reduced by employing automatic function prediction (AFP) approaches. Gene Ontology (GO), comprising of more than 40, 000 classes, defines three aspects of protein function names Biological Process (BP), Cellular Component (CC), Molecular Function (MF). Multiple functions of a single protein, has made automatic function prediction a large-scale, multi-class, multi-label task. In this paper, we present DEEPGONET, a novel cascaded convolutional and recurrent neural network, to predict the top-level hierarchy of GO ontology. The network takes the primary sequence of protein as input which makes it more useful than other prevailing state-of-the-art deep learning based methods with multi-modal input, making them less applicable for proteins where only primary sequence is available. All predictions of different protein functions of our network are performed by the same architecture, a proof of better generalization as demonstrated by promising performance on a variety of organisms while trained on Homo sapiens only, which is made possible by efficient exploration of vast output space by leveraging hierarchical relationship among GO classes. The promising performance of our model makes it a potential avenue for directing experimental protein functions exploration efficiently by vastly eliminating possible routes which is done by the exploring only the suggested routes from our model. Our proposed model is also very simple and efficient in terms of computational time and space compared to other architectures in literature.
raw_candidates.title: DEEPGONET: Multi-label Prediction of GO Annotation for Protein from Sequence Using Cascaded Convolutional and Recurrent Network

metadata.query_text: protein function prediction Gene Ontology multi-label degradation low annotation ratio 1% 10% baseline

raw_candidates.snippet: Exploring the functions of genes and gene products is crucial to a wide range of fields, including medical research, evolutionary biology, and environmental science. However, discovering new functions largely relies on expensive and exhaustive wet lab experiments. Existing methods of automatic function annotation or prediction mainly focus on protein function prediction with sequence, 3D-structures or protein family information. In this study, we propose to tackle the gene function prediction problem by exploring Gene Ontology graph and annotation with BERT (GoBERT) to decipher the underlying relationships among gene functions. Our proposed novel function prediction task utilizes existing functions as inputs and generalizes the function prediction to gene and gene products. Specifically, two pre-train tasks are designed to jointly train GoBERT to capture both explicit and implicit relations of functions. Neighborhood prediction is a self-supervised multi-label classification task that captures the explicit function relations. Specified masking and recovering task helps GoBERT in finding implicit patterns among functions. The pre-trained GoBERT possess the ability to predict novel functions for various gene and gene products based on known functional annotations. Extensive experiments, biological case studies, and ablation studies are conducted to demonstrate the superiority of our proposed GoBERT.
raw_candidates.title: GoBERT: Gene Ontology Graph Informed BERT for Universal Gene Function Prediction

The study reports that GoBERT demonstrates superiority over existing methods through extensive experiments, biological case studies, and ablation studies.

metadata.query_text: "PANNs Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Pattern Recognition" official implementation code repository

items.summary: Audio pattern recognition is an important research topic in the machine learning area, and includes several tasks such as audio tagging, acoustic scene classification, music classification, speech emotion classification and sound event detection. Recently, neural networks have been applied to tackle audio pattern recognition problems. However, previous systems are built on specific datasets with limited durations. Recently, in computer vision and natural language processing, systems pretrained on large-scale datasets have generalized well to several tasks. However, there is limited research on pretraining systems on large-scale datasets for audio pattern recognition. In this paper, we propose pretrained audio neural networks (PANNs) trained on the large-scale AudioSet dataset. These PANNs are transferred to other audio related tasks. We investigate the performance and computational complexity of PANNs modeled by a variety of convolutional neural networks. We propose an architecture called Wavegram-Logmel-CNN using both log-mel spectrogram and waveform as input feature. Our best PANN system achieves a state-of-the-art mean average precision (mAP) of 0.439 on AudioSet tagging, outperforming the best previous system of 0.392. We transfer PANNs to six audio pattern recognition tasks, and demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in several of those tasks. We have released the source code and pretrained models of PANNs: https://github.com/qiuqiangkong/audioset_tagging_cnn.
items.title: PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition

supported_claims: PANNs are pretrained audio neural networks trained on the large-scale AudioSet dataset.
The PANNs system achieves a state-of-the-art mean average precision (mAP) of 0.439 on AudioSet tagging, outperforming the previous best system with 0.392 mAP.
The PANNs paper investigates the performance and computational complexity of PANNs modeled by various convolutional neural networks, including the proposed Wavegram-Logmel-CNN architecture.
The PANNs paper demonstrates transfer learning to six audio pattern recognition tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance in several of them.
The PANNs paper and its linked repository provide the source code and pretrained models of PANNs, enabling reproducibility and further experimentation.
evidence.title: PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition

supported_claims: The PANNs paper and its linked repository provide the source code and pretrained models of PANNs, enabling reproducibility and further experimentation.
evidence.title: qiuqiangkong/audioset_tagging_cnn

hypothesis.condition: The method targets low SNR (< 10 dB) and device domain shift scenarios in industrial machine anomaly sound recognition tasks, specifically on the MIMII dataset.
hypothesis.guardrail: Ensure that noise injection and whitening parameters are derived from a held-out validation set to prevent data leakage and maintain evaluation integrity.
hypothesis.intervention: Add a domain-adaptive feature augmentation module that injects device-specific noise into PANNs features and applies whitening to align feature statistics across devices, enabling robust few-shot learning under low SNR and domain shift.
hypothesis.limitation: The method assumes access to representative noise profiles and device-specific statistics for whitening; performance may degrade if these are not representative of the target domain.
hypothesis.mechanism: The domain-adaptive feature augmentation module addresses domain shift by aligning feature distributions across devices and noise conditions, thereby improving the effectiveness of few-shot learning on the pretrained PANNs features.
hypothesis.predicted_metric_change: Expected improvement in few-shot anomaly detection performance (e.g., AUC or F1) on MIMII under low SNR and device domain shift conditions compared to the baseline PANNs without augmentation.

research.intended_claim: Introduce a domain-adaptive feature augmentation module that combines SNR-aware noise injection and device-specific feature whitening. The module is switchable and operates as a post-pretraining intervention on top of the frozen PANNs backbone. It generates synthetic few-shot training samples by mixing clean PANNs features with device-specific noise profiles and applies whitening to align feature statistics across devices.
research.observed_problem: The method assumes access to representative noise profiles and device-specific statistics for whitening; performance may degrade if these are not representative of the target domain.
research.proposed_mechanism: The domain-adaptive feature augmentation module addresses domain shift by aligning feature distributions across devices and noise conditions, thereby improving the effectiveness of few-shot learning on the pretrained PANNs features.
research.scientific_setting: 面向工业机器声音异常检测任务，聚焦于低信噪比环境（SNR < 10 dB）和设备域偏移（跨设备泛化）场景。方法设计需基于 PANNs 的预训练模型，并采用少样本学习（few-shot learning）策略，在 MIMII 数据集上进行验证。
research.success_metric: Few-shot anomaly detection performance (e.g., AUC or F1) on the MIMII dataset,
research.target_problem: 以论文《PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition》为 Baseline，在 MIMII 工业机器声音存在低信噪比和设备域偏移时，设计少样本异常声音识别方法。

method.problem_method_insight: PANNs leverage large-scale pretraining on AudioSet to capture robust audio representations, but their performance degrades under low SNR and device domain shift conditions, particularly in few-shot settings where labeled data is scarce. The failure mechanism likely stems from domain mismatch between pretraining (general audio) and target (industrial machine sounds) and noise sensitivity of the pretrained features.

plan.problem_statement: 设计一种在低信噪比（SNR）和设备域偏移（device domain shift）条件下，能够在少样本（few-shot）场景下有效识别工业机器异常声音的方法。该方法需以论文《PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition》作为基线模型，并通过实验验证在 MIMII 数据集上的性能提升。
research_questions: 在低信噪比和设备域偏移条件下，PANNs 基线模型的性能下降幅度有多大？
哪些少样本学习方法（如元学习、迁移学习、自监督预训练等）能够有效缓解低信噪比和设备域偏移问题？
如何设计适配 PANNs 的少样本异常声音识别方法，以在 MIMII 数据集上实现显著性能提升？
在少样本设置下，所提方法的泛化能力（跨设备、跨噪声类型）如何？
risks: PANNs 的基线性能在低信噪比和设备域偏移下可能不满足少样本学习的前提条件，导致方法设计受限。
MIMII 数据集的官方评估协议或设备域偏移特征可能存在未公开细节，影响方法设计与评估的一致性。
少样本学习方法的选择需与 PANNs 的预训练特性高度匹配，否则可能无法有效缓解低信噪比和设备域偏移问题。
缺乏明确的少样本样本数量定义（如 1-shot、5-shot 等），需在实验中进行消融研究以确定最佳样本规模。
plan.scope: 面向工业机器声音异常检测任务，聚焦于低信噪比环境（SNR < 10 dB）和设备域偏移（跨设备泛化）场景。方法设计需基于 PANNs 的预训练模型，并采用少样本学习（few-shot learning）策略，在 MIMII 数据集上进行验证。

metadata.query_text: "PANNs Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Pattern Recognition"

raw_candidates.snippet: Audio pattern recognition is an important research topic in the machine learning area, and includes several tasks such as audio tagging, acoustic scene classification, music classification, speech emotion classification and sound event detection. Recently, neural networks have been applied to tackle audio pattern recognition problems. However, previous systems are built on specific datasets with limited durations. Recently, in computer vision and natural language processing, systems pretrained on large-scale datasets have generalized well to several tasks. However, there is limited research on pretraining systems on large-scale datasets for audio pattern recognition. In this paper, we propose pretrained audio neural networks (PANNs) trained on the large-scale AudioSet dataset. These PANNs are transferred to other audio related tasks. We investigate the performance and computational complexity of PANNs modeled by a variety of convolutional neural networks. We propose an architecture called Wavegram-Logmel-CNN using both log-mel spectrogram and waveform as input feature. Our best PANN system achieves a state-of-the-art mean average precision (mAP) of 0.439 on AudioSet tagging, outperforming the best previous system of 0.392. We transfer PANNs to six audio pattern recognition tasks, and demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in several of those tasks. We have released the source code and pretrained models of PANNs: https://github.com/qiuqiangkong/audioset_tagging_cnn.
raw_candidates.title: PANNs: Large-Scale Pretrained Audio Neural Networks for Audio Pattern Recognition

gap_assessments.summary: The PANNs paper and its linked repository explicitly provide the identity of the baseline model and its official implementation code repository.

The PANNs paper demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on AudioSet tagging with a mean average precision (mAP) of 0.439, outperforming the previous best system (0.392 mAP), indicating strong baseline capabilities for audio pattern recognition tasks.

The PANNs paper investigates the performance and computational complexity of various convolutional neural network architectures, including the proposed Wavegram-Logmel-CNN, which uses both log-mel spectrogram and waveform as input features.

The PANNs paper demonstrates transfer learning to six audio pattern recognition tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance in several of them, supporting the feasibility of adapting PANNs to new tasks.
The PANNs paper and its linked repository provide the source code and pretrained models, enabling reproducibility and further experimentation on audio pattern recognition tasks.

No evidence is provided regarding the performance of PANNs or its adaptations in low SNR conditions, device domain shift scenarios, or few-shot learning settings on industrial machine anomaly sound recognition tasks such as MIMII.

limitations: The provided evidence does not address the performance of PANNs or its adaptations in low SNR conditions, device domain shift, or few-shot learning scenarios for industrial machine anomaly sound recognition.
The PANNs paper focuses on general audio pattern recognition tasks and does not provide specific evaluations on datasets like MIMII.
The evidence does not include any experimental results or methods for addressing the stated problem (low SNR, device domain shift, few-shot learning) beyond the baseline PANNs model.

verified_findings.text: PANNs are pretrained audio neural networks trained on the large-scale AudioSet dataset.
The PANNs system achieves a state-of-the-art mean average precision (mAP) of 0.439 on AudioSet tagging, outperforming the previous best system with 0.392 mAP.
The PANNs paper investigates the performance and computational complexity of PANNs modeled by various convolutional neural networks, including the proposed Wavegram-Logmel-CNN architecture.
The PANNs paper demonstrates transfer learning to six audio pattern recognition tasks, achieving state-of-the-art performance in several of them.
The PANNs paper and its linked repository provide the source code and pretrained models of PANNs, enabling reproducibility and further experimentation.
